1.arabic: بِسْمِ ٱللَّهِ ٱلرَّحْمَـٰنِ ٱلرَّحِيمِ يَسْـَٔلُونَكَ عَنِ ٱلْأَنفَالِ ۖ قُلِ ٱلْأَنفَالُ لِلَّهِ وَٱلرَّسُولِ ۖ فَٱتَّقُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ وَأَصْلِحُوا۟ ذَاتَ بَيْنِكُمْ ۖ وَأَطِيعُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ وَرَسُولَهُۥٓ إِن كُنتُم مُّؤْمِنِينَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: THEY ASK YOU of (benefits accruing as) spoils of war. Tell them: "The benefits belong to God and His Messenger." So fulfil your duty to God and keep peace among yourselves. Obey God and the Prophet, If you really believe.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: They ask you O dear Prophet (Mohammed – peace and blessings be upon him) concerning the war booty; say, “Allah and the Noble Messenger are the owners of the war booty; so fear Allah and maintain friendship among yourselves; and obey Allah and His Noble Messenger, if you have faith."

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: They will question thee concerning the spoils. Say: 'The spoils belong to God and the Messenger; so fear you God, and set things right between you, and obey you God and His Messenger, if you are believers.'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: They ask thee concerning the spoils of war. Say thou: the spoils of war are Allah's and the apostle's. So fear Allah, and set right the matter among you, and obey Allah and His apostle if ye are believers.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: They ask you (O Muhammad SAW) about the spoils of war. Say: "The spoils are for Allah and the Messenger." So fear Allah and adjust all matters of difference among you, and obey Allah and His Messenger (Muhammad SAW), if you are believers.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: They ask you about the bounties. Say, “The bounties are for God and the Messenger.” So be mindful of God, and settle your differences, and obey God and His Messenger, if you are believers.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: They ask you concerning the spoils of war? Tell them: 'The spoils of war belong to Allah and the Messenger. So fear Allah, and set things right between you, and obey Allah and His Messenger if you are true believers.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: They ask you about Al-Anfal (the spoils of war). Say: "Al-Anfal are for Allah and the Messenger." So have Taqwa of Allah and settle all matters of difference among you, and obey Allah and His Messenger, if you are believers.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: They ask thee (O Muhammad) of the spoils of war. Say: The spoils of war belong to Allah and the messenger, so keep your duty to Allah, and adjust the matter of your difference, and obey Allah and His messenger, if ye are (true) believers.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: They ask you concerning the anfaal. Say, ‘The anfaal belong to Allah and the Apostle.’ So be wary of Allah and settle your differences, and obey Allah and His Apostle, should you be faithful.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: They ask you about the spoils (of war). Say: 'The spoils belong to Allah and the Messenger. Therefore, have fear of Allah and set things right between you. Obey Allah and His Messenger, if you are believers'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: They ask you, [O Muhammad], about the bounties [of war]. Say, "The [decision concerning] bounties is for Allah and the Messenger." So fear Allah and amend that which is between you and obey Allah and His Messenger, if you should be believers.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: They (the believers) ask you (Muhammad) about the booty captured (from the enemies) during a war. Tell them, "It belongs to God and the Messengers. If you have faith, have fear of God. Settle the disputes among yourselves and obey God and His Messengers."

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: They ask you about the windfalls. Say: The windfalls are for Allah and the Apostle. So be careful of (your duty to) Allah and set aright matters of your difference, and obey Allah and His Apostle if you are believers.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: They ask you about the spoils of war. Say, "They belong to God and His Messenger. So fear God, and set things right among yourselves, and obey God and His Messenger, if you are true believers:

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: They ask thee concerning (things taken as) spoils of war. Say: "(such) spoils are at the disposal of Allah and the Messenger: So fear Allah, and keep straight the relations between yourselves: Obey Allah and His Messenger, if ye do believe."

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Yasaloonaka AAani alanfali quli alanfalu lillahi waalrrasooli faittaqoo Allaha waaslihoo thata baynikum waateeAAoo Allaha warasoolahu in kuntum mumineena

2.arabic: إِنَّمَا ٱلْمُؤْمِنُونَ ٱلَّذِينَ إِذَا ذُكِرَ ٱللَّهُ وَجِلَتْ قُلُوبُهُمْ وَإِذَا تُلِيَتْ عَلَيْهِمْ ءَايَـٰتُهُۥ زَادَتْهُمْ إِيمَـٰنًا وَعَلَىٰ رَبِّهِمْ يَتَوَكَّلُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Only they are true believers whose hearts fill up with awe when the name of God is mentioned; and their faith is further strengthened when His messages are read out to them; and those who place their trust in their Lord,

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Only they are the believers whose hearts fear when Allah is remembered, and their faith advances when His verses are recited to them, and who trust only in their Lord.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Those only are believers who, when God is mentioned, their hearts quake, and when His signs are recited to them, it increases them in faith, and in their Lord they put their trust,

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: The believers are only those whose hearts thrill with fear when Allah is mentioned, and when His revelations are rehearsed unto them, they increase their faith and who trust in their Lord.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: The believers are only those who, when Allah is mentioned, feel a fear in their hearts and when His Verses (this Quran) are recited unto them, they (i.e. the Verses) increase their Faith; and they put their trust in their Lord (Alone);

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: The believers are those whose hearts tremble when God is mentioned, and when His revelations are recited to them, they strengthen them in faith, and upon their Lord they rely.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: The true believers are those who, when Allah's name is mentioned, their hearts quake, and when His verses are recited to them their faith grows, and who put their trust in their Lord;

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: The believers are only those who, when Allah is mentioned, feel a fear in their hearts and when His Ayat are recited unto them, they increase their faith; and they put their trust in their Lord;

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: They only are the (true) believers whose hearts feel fear when Allah is mentioned, and when His revelations are recited unto them they increase their faith, and who trust in their Lord;

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: The faithful are only those whose hearts tremble [with awe] when Allah is mentioned, and when His signs are recited to them, they increase their faith, and who put their trust in their Lord,

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Indeed the believers are those whose hearts quake at the mention of Allah, and when His verses are recited to them it increased them in faith. They are those who put their trust in their Lord.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: The believers are only those who, when Allah is mentioned, their hearts become fearful, and when His verses are recited to them, it increases them in faith; and upon their Lord they rely -

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: When God is mentioned, the true believers begin to feel fear of Him in their hearts and when His revelations are recited to them their faith strengthens. In God alone do they trust.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Those only are believers whose hearts become full of fear when Allah is mentioned, and when His communications are recited to them they increase them in faith, and in their Lord do they trust.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: true believers are those whose hearts tremble with awe at the mention of God, and whose faith grows stronger as they listen to His revelations. They are those who put their trust in their Lord,

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: For, Believers are those who, when Allah is mentioned, feel a tremor in their hearts, and when they hear His signs rehearsed, find their faith strengthened, and put (all) their trust in their Lord;

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Innama almuminoona allatheena itha thukira Allahu wajilat quloobuhum waitha tuliyat AAalayhim ayatuhu zadathum eemanan waAAala rabbihim yatawakkaloona

3.arabic: ٱلَّذِينَ يُقِيمُونَ ٱلصَّلَوٰةَ وَمِمَّا رَزَقْنَـٰهُمْ يُنفِقُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Who are firm in devotion, and spend of what We have given them,

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Those who keep the prayer established and spend in Our cause from what We have bestowed upon them.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: those who perform the prayer, and expend of what We have provided them,

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Who establish prayer and who expend of that wherewith We have provided them.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Who perform As-Salat (Iqamat-as-Salat) and spend out of that We have provided them.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Those who perform the prayer; and from Our provisions to them, they spend.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: who establish Prayer and spend out of what We have provided them.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Who perform the Salah and spend out of what We have provided them.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Who establish worship and spend of that We have bestowed on them.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: maintain the prayer and spend out of what We have provided them.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Those who pray steadfastly, and spend of that which We have provided them,

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: The ones who establish prayer, and from what We have provided them, they spend.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: They are steadfast in prayer and spend part of what We have given them for the cause of God.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Those who keep up prayer and spend (benevolently) out of what We have given them.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: who pray regularly and give in alms out of what We have provided for them.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Who establish regular prayers and spend (freely) out of the gifts We have given them for sustenance:

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Allatheena yuqeemoona alssalata wamimma razaqnahum yunfiqoona

4.arabic: أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ هُمُ ٱلْمُؤْمِنُونَ حَقًّا ۚ لَّهُمْ دَرَجَـٰتٌ عِندَ رَبِّهِمْ وَمَغْفِرَةٌ وَرِزْقٌ كَرِيمٌ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Are true believers. There are for them (high) ranks with their Lord, and pardon and noble provision.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: These are the true Muslims; for them are ranks before their Lord, and forgiveness and an honourable sustenance.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: those in truth are the believers; they have degrees with their Lord, and forgiveness, and generous provision.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Those: it is they who are the true believers. For them are degrees with their Lord and forgiveness and a provision honourable.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: It is they who are the believers in truth. For them are grades of dignity with their Lord, and Forgiveness and a generous provision (Paradise).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: These are the true believers. They have high standing with their Lord, and forgiveness, and a generous provision.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Such people are indeed true believers. They have high ranks with their Lord, and forgiveness for their sins and an honourable sustenance.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: It is they who are the believers in truth. For them are grades of dignity with their Lord, and forgiveness and a generous provision (Paradise).

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Those are they who are in truth believers. For them are grades (of honour) with their Lord, and pardon, and a bountiful provision.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: It is they who are truly the faithful. They shall have ranks near their Lord, forgiveness and a noble provision.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: those are, in truth, the believers. They shall have degrees with their Lord and forgiveness, and a generous provision.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Those are the believers, truly. For them are degrees [of high position] with their Lord and forgiveness and noble provision.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Such are the true believers. Their reward from their Lord will be high ranks, forgiveness, and a generous provision.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: These are the believers in truth; they shall have from their Lord exalted grades and forgiveness and an honorable sustenance.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Such are the true believers. They have a high standing with their Lord, His forgiveness and an honourable provision made for them.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Such in truth are the believers: they have grades of dignity with their Lord, and forgiveness, and generous sustenance:

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Olaika humu almuminoona haqqan lahum darajatun AAinda rabbihim wamaghfiratun warizqun kareemun

5.arabic: كَمَآ أَخْرَجَكَ رَبُّكَ مِنۢ بَيْتِكَ بِٱلْحَقِّ وَإِنَّ فَرِيقًا مِّنَ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ لَكَـٰرِهُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: As your Lord sent you from your home (to fight) for the true cause, a section of the faithful were averse,

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: The way your Lord caused you, O dear Prophet to come forth from your home with the truth; and indeed a group of Muslims were unhappy about it.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: As thy Lord brought thee forth from thy house with the truth, and a part of the believers were averse to it,

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: This is like what time thy Lord had caused thee to go forthfrom thy house for a right cause, while a party among the believers were averse.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: As your Lord caused you (O Muhammad SAW) to go out from your home with the truth, and verily, a party among the believers disliked it;

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Even as your Lord brought you out of your home with the truth, some believers were reluctant.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: (Now with regard to the spoils the same situation exists as when) your Lord brought you forth from your home in a righteous cause while a party among the believers were much averse to it.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: As your Lord caused you to go out from your home with the truth; and verily, a party among the believers disliked it.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Even as thy Lord caused thee (Muhammad) to go forth from thy home with the Truth, and lo! a party of the believers were averse (to it).

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: As your Lord brought you out from your home with a just cause, a part of the faithful were indeed reluctant.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: It is like when your Lord caused you to leave your home with the truth, though some of the believers disliked it.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: [It is] just as when your Lord brought you out of your home [for the battle of Badr] in truth, while indeed, a party among the believers were unwilling,

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: A group among the believers dislike (God's decree about the booty captured in war) as well as His command that you come out of your home for a truthful purpose (to fight for justice).

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Even as your Lord caused you to go forth from your house with the truth, though a party of the believers were surely averse;

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: As it was your Lord who rightfully brought you forth from your house, even though some of the believers disliked it,

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Just as thy Lord ordered thee out of thy house in truth, even though a party among the Believers disliked it,

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Kama akhrajaka rabbuka min baytika bialhaqqi wainna fareeqan mina almumineena lakarihoona

6.arabic: يُجَـٰدِلُونَكَ فِى ٱلْحَقِّ بَعْدَ مَا تَبَيَّنَ كَأَنَّمَا يُسَاقُونَ إِلَى ٱلْمَوْتِ وَهُمْ يَنظُرُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Who argued with you about the matter even after it had become quite clear, as if they were being pushed into (the arms of) death as they waited.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Disputing with you regarding the truth after it had been made clear, as if they were being herded towards a visible death.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: disputing with thee concerning the truth after it had become clear, as though they were being driven into death with their eyes wide open.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Disputing with thee respecting the right cause after it had become manifest, as though they were led forth unto death while they looked on.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Disputing with you concerning the truth after it was made manifest, as if they were being driven to death, while they were looking (at it).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Arguing with you about the truth after it was made clear, as if they were being driven to death as they looked on.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: They disputed with you about the truth after that had become evident, as if they were being driven to death with their eyes wide open.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Disputing with you concerning the truth after it was made manifest, as if they were being driven to death, while they were looking (at it).

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Disputing with thee of the Truth after it had been made manifest, as if they were being driven to death visible.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: They disputed with you concerning the truth after it had become clear, as if they were being driven towards death as they looked on.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: They argued with you about the truth after it had been made clear, as though they were being led to certain death while looking.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Arguing with you concerning the truth after it had become clear, as if they were being driven toward death while they were looking on.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Knowing it well (that a fight in which they are to take part is about to take place), they act as though they are being driven to death which they can see before their very eyes. Despite their knowledge of the truth, they still argue with you.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: They disputed with you about the truth after it had become clear, (and they went forth) as if they were being driven to death while they saw (it).

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: and they disputed with you concerning the truth after it had become manifest, as though they were being driven to their death with open eyes.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Disputing with thee concerning the truth after it was made manifest, as if they were being driven to death and they (actually) saw it.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Yujadiloonaka fee alhaqqi baAAdama tabayyana kaannama yusaqoona ila almawti wahum yanthuroona

7.arabic: وَإِذْ يَعِدُكُمُ ٱللَّهُ إِحْدَى ٱلطَّآئِفَتَيْنِ أَنَّهَا لَكُمْ وَتَوَدُّونَ أَنَّ غَيْرَ ذَاتِ ٱلشَّوْكَةِ تَكُونُ لَكُمْ وَيُرِيدُ ٱللَّهُ أَن يُحِقَّ ٱلْحَقَّ بِكَلِمَـٰتِهِۦ وَيَقْطَعَ دَابِرَ ٱلْكَـٰفِرِينَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Though God promised that one of two columns (would fall to you), you desired the one that was not armed. But God wished to confirm the truth by His words, and wipe the unbelievers out to the last,

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And remember when Allah promised you that one of the two groups (of enemies) is for you, and you wished to get the one that posed no danger, and Allah willed to prove the truth with His Words, and to cut the origins of the disbelievers.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: And when God promised you one of the two parties should be yours, and you were wishing that the one not accoutred should be yours; but God was desiring to verify the truth by His words, and to cut off the unbelievers to the last remnant,

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And recall what time Allah was promising you one of the two parties that it should be yours, and ye would fain to have that the one without; Whilst Allah besought arms were yours to justify the truth by His words and to cut off the root of the infidels.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And (remember) when Allah promised you (Muslims) one of the two parties (of the enemy i.e. either the army or the caravan) that it should be yours, you wished that the one not armed (the caravan) should be yours, but Allah willed to justify the truth by His Words and to cut off the roots of the disbelievers (i.e. in the battle of Badr).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: God has promised you one of the two groups—that it would be yours—but you wanted the unarmed group to be yours. God intends to prove the truth with His words, and to uproot the disbelievers.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: And recall when Allah promised you that one of the two hosts would fall to you, and you wished that the one without arms should fall into your hands. But Allah sought to prove by His words the truth to be true and to annihilate the unbelievers to the last remnant

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And (remember) when Allah promised you (Muslims) one of the two parties, that it should be yours; you wished that the one not armed should be yours, but Allah willed to justify the truth by His Words and to cut off the roots of the disbelievers.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And when Allah promised you one of the two bands (of the enemy) that it should be yours, and ye longed that other than the armed one might be yours. And Allah willed that He should cause the Truth to triumph by His words, and cut the root of the disbelievers;

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: When Allah promised you [victory over] one of the two companies, [saying], ‘It is for you,’ you were eager that it should be the one that was unarmed. But Allah desires to confirm the truth with His words, and to root out the faithless,

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: (Remember) when Allah promised to grant you one of the two parties (at Badr), and you wished for the one that was not strong. Allah wanted to establish the truth by His Words and to sever the unbelievers to the last,

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: [Remember, O believers], when Allah promised you one of the two groups - that it would be yours - and you wished that the unarmed one would be yours. But Allah intended to establish the truth by His words and to eliminate the disbelievers

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: When God promised to grant you (believers) victory over either one of the two groups, you wished to have control over the unarmed one. God decided to prove (to you) the truth of His promises and to destroy the unbelievers

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And when Allah promised you one of the two parties that it shall be yours and you loved that the one not armed should he yours and Allah desired to manifest the truth of what was true by His words and to cut off the root of the unbelievers.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: God promised you that one of the two parties would fall to you, and you wished that the one without sting should be yours, but God wanted to establish the truth by His words and cut off the root of those who denied the truth --

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Behold! Allah promised you one of the two (enemy) parties, that it should be yours: Ye wished that the one unarmed should be yours, but Allah willed to justify the Truth according to His words and to cut off the roots of the Unbelievers;-

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Waith yaAAidukumu Allahu ihda alttaifatayni annaha lakum watawaddoona anna ghayra thati alshshawkati takoonu lakum wayureedu Allahu an yuhiqqa alhaqqa bikalimatihi wayaqtaAAa dabira alkafireena

8.arabic: لِيُحِقَّ ٱلْحَقَّ وَيُبْطِلَ ٱلْبَـٰطِلَ وَلَوْ كَرِهَ ٱلْمُجْرِمُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: So that Truth may be affirmed and falsehood negated, even though the sinners be averse.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: In order that He may prove the truth and disprove falsehood, even if the criminals get annoyed.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: and that He might verify the truth and prove untrue the untrue, though the sinners were averse to it.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: In order that he might justify the truth and falsify the false even though the guilty ones were averse.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: That He might cause the truth to triumph and bring falsehood to nothing, even though the Mujrimun (disbelievers, polytheists, sinners, criminals, etc.) hate it.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: In order to confirm the truth and nullify falsehood, even though the guilty dislike it.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: that He might prove the truth to be true and the false to be false, however averse the evil-doers might be to it.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: That He might cause the truth to triumph and bring falsehood to nothing, even though the criminals hate it.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: That He might cause the Truth to triumph and bring vanity to naught, however much the guilty might oppose;

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: so that He may confirm the truth and bring falsehood to naught, though the guilty should be averse.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: in order that He might verify the truth and annul the falsehood, even though the wrongdoers hated it.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: That He should establish the truth and abolish falsehood, even if the criminals disliked it.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: so that the truth would stand supreme and falsehood would exist no more, even though the criminals dislike it.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: That He may manifest the truth of what was true and show the falsehood of what was false, though the guilty disliked.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: so that He might prove the truth to be true and the false to be false, however much the wrongdoers might dislike it.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: That He might justify Truth and prove Falsehood false, distasteful though it be to those in guilt.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Liyuhiqqa alhaqqa wayubtila albatila walaw kariha almujrimoona

9.arabic: إِذْ تَسْتَغِيثُونَ رَبَّكُمْ فَٱسْتَجَابَ لَكُمْ أَنِّى مُمِدُّكُم بِأَلْفٍ مِّنَ ٱلْمَلَـٰٓئِكَةِ مُرْدِفِينَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Remember when you prayed to your Lord for help, He heard you and said: "I shall send a thousand angels following behind you for your aid."

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: When you (Prophet Mohammed – peace and blessings be upon him) were seeking the help of your Lord, so He answered your prayers that, “I will help you with a row of thousands of angels.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: When you were calling upon your Lord for succour, and He answered you, 'I shall reinforce you with a thousand angels riding behind you.'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And recall what time ye implored our Lord and He answered you: verily I am about to succour you with a thousand of angels rank in rank.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: (Remember) when you sought help of your Lord and He answered you (saying): "I will help you with a thousand of the angels each behind the other (following one another) in succession."

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: When you appealed to your Lord for help, He answered you, “I am reinforcing you with one thousand angels in succession.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: And recall when you implored your Lord for help and He responded to you: 'I will indeed reinforce you with a thousand angels, coming host after host.'

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: (Remember) when you sought help of your Lord and He answered you (saying): "I will help you with a thousand of the angels Murdifin."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: When ye sought help of your Lord and He answered you (saying): I will help you with a thousand of the angels, rank on rank.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: When you appealed to your Lord for help, He answered you: ‘I will aid you with a thousand angels in a file.’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: And when you (Prophet Muhammad) prayed to your Lord for help, He answered: 'I am sending to your aid a thousand angels in succession'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: [Remember] when you asked help of your Lord, and He answered you, "Indeed, I will reinforce you with a thousand from the angels, following one another."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: When you (believers) begged for assistance from your Lord, He said, "I am helping you with a thousand angels, all in rows marching one after the other".

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: When you sought aid from your Lord, so He answered you: I will assist you with a thousand of the angels following one another.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: When you prayed to your Lord for help, He answered, "I am sending to your aid a thousand angels in succession."

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Remember ye implored the assistance of your Lord, and He answered you: "I will assist you with a thousand of the angels, ranks on ranks."

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Ith tastagheethoona rabbakum faistajaba lakum annee mumiddukum bialfin mina almalaikati murdifeena

10.arabic: وَمَا جَعَلَهُ ٱللَّهُ إِلَّا بُشْرَىٰ وَلِتَطْمَئِنَّ بِهِۦ قُلُوبُكُمْ ۚ وَمَا ٱلنَّصْرُ إِلَّا مِنْ عِندِ ٱللَّهِ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ عَزِيزٌ حَكِيمٌ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: He gave you the good news only to reassure your hearts, for victory comes from God alone, and certainly God is all-mighty and all-wise.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And Allah did this just for your happiness and for your hearts to gain contentment; and help does not come except from Allah; indeed Allah is Almighty, Wise.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: God wrought this not, save as good tidings and that your hearts thereby might be at rest; help comes only from God; surely God is All-mighty, All-wise.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And Allah made not this save as a glad tidings, and that your hearts might thereby be set at rest; and succour cometh not but from Allah. Verily Allah is Mighty, Wise.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Allah made it only as glad tidings, and that your hearts be at rest therewith. And there is no victory except from Allah. Verily, Allah is All-Mighty, All-Wise.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: God only made it a message of hope, and to set your hearts at rest. Victory comes only from God. God is Mighty and Wise.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Allah meant this as glad tidings and that your hearts may be set at rest. For every help comes from Allah alone. Surely Allah is All-Mighty, All-Wise.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Allah made it only as glad tidings, and that your hearts be at rest therewith. And there is no victory except from Allah. Verily, Allah is Almighty, All-Wise.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Allah appointed it only as good tidings, and that your hearts thereby might be at rest. Victory cometh only by the help of Allah. Lo! Allah is Mighty, Wise.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Allah did not appoint it but as a good news, and to reassure your hearts. Victory comes only from Allah. Indeed Allah is all-mighty, all-wise.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Allah only made it to be glad tidings and so that your hearts may be satisfied; victory comes only from Allah; He is Almighty, Wise.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And Allah made it not but good tidings and so that your hearts would be assured thereby. And victory is not but from Allah. Indeed, Allah is Exalted in Might and Wise.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: God has sent this glad news to comfort your hearts. Victory is in the hands of God alone. God is Majestic and All-wise.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And Allah only gave it as a good news and that your hearts might be at ease thereby; and victory is only from Allah; surely Allah is Mighty, Wise.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: God only did this to give you good news, and so that your hearts might be set at rest, for help comes from God alone. Surely, God is Mighty and Wise.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Allah made it but a message of hope, and an assurance to your hearts: (in any case) there is no help except from Allah: and Allah is Exalted in Power, Wise.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Wama jaAAalahu Allahu illa bushra walitatmainna bihi quloobukum wama alnnasru illa min AAindi Allahi inna Allaha AAazeezun hakeemun

11.arabic: إِذْ يُغَشِّيكُمُ ٱلنُّعَاسَ أَمَنَةً مِّنْهُ وَيُنَزِّلُ عَلَيْكُم مِّنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ مَآءً لِّيُطَهِّرَكُم بِهِۦ وَيُذْهِبَ عَنكُمْ رِجْزَ ٱلشَّيْطَـٰنِ وَلِيَرْبِطَ عَلَىٰ قُلُوبِكُمْ وَيُثَبِّتَ بِهِ ٱلْأَقْدَامَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: A blanketing sleep came over you as a (measure of) security from Him, and He sent down rain from the skies to cleanse you, and to remove the plague of Satan, and to strengthen your hearts and steady your steps.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: When He made the slumber overcome you, so it was as a peacefulness from Him, and sent down water from the sky upon you to purify you with it, and to remove the impurity of Satan from you, and to give your hearts fortitude and firmly establish your feet with it.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: When He was causing slumber to overcome you as a security from Him, and sending down on you water from heaven, to purify you thereby, and to put away from you the defilement of Satan, and to strengthen your hearts, and to confirm your feet.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Recall what time He caused slumber to cover you as a security from Himself, and He sent down rain upon you from heaven that he might cleanse you thereby and take away from you the pollution of the Satan, and that he might gird up your hearts and make your feet firm thereby.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: (Remember) when He covered you with a slumber as a security from Him, and He caused water (rain) to descend on you from the sky, to clean you thereby and to remove from you the Rijz (whispering, evil-suggestions, etc.) of Shaitan (Satan), and to strengthen your hearts, and make your feet firm thereby.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: He made drowsiness overcome you, as a security from Him. And He sent down upon you water from the sky, to cleanse you with it, and to rid you of Satan’s pollution, and to fortify your hearts, and to strengthen your foothold.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: And recall when Allah brought on you drowsiness, giving you a feeling of peace and security from Him, and He sent down rain upon you from the sky that He might cleanse you through it and take away from you the pollution of Satan and strengthen your hearts, and steady your feet through it.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: (Remember) when He covered you with a slumber as a security from Him, and He caused water (rain) to descend on you from the sky, to clean you thereby and to remove from you the Rijz (whispering or dirt) of Shaytan, and to strengthen your hearts, and make your feet firm thereby.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: When He made the slumber fall upon you as a reassurance from him and sent down water from the sky upon you, that thereby He might purify you, and remove from you the fear of Satan, and make strong your hearts and firm (your) feet thereby.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: When He covered you with a trance as a [sense of] security from Him, and He sent down water from the sky to purify you with it, and to repel from you the defilement of Satan, and to fortify your hearts, and to make [your] feet steady with it.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: When you were overcome by sleep, as security from Him, He sent down water from the sky to cleanse you and to purify you of satan's filth, to strengthen your hearts and to steady your footsteps.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: [Remember] when He overwhelmed you with drowsiness [giving] security from Him and sent down upon you from the sky, rain by which to purify you and remove from you the evil [suggestions] of Satan and to make steadfast your hearts and plant firmly thereby your feet.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: The slumber that overcame you was from God that brought you peace. He showered water from the sky over you to clean you, remove satanic wickedness from you, strengthen your hearts, and grant you steadfastness.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: When He caused calm to fall on you as a security from Him and sent down upon you water from the cloud that He might thereby purify you, and take away from you the uncleanness of the Shaitan, and that He might fortify your hearts and steady (your) footsteps thereby.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: He brought drowsiness upon you to give you His reassurance and sent down water from the sky upon you, so that He might thereby purify you and remove Satan's pollution from you, and make your hearts strong and your feet firm.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Remember He covered you with a sort of drowsiness, to give you calm as from Himself, and he caused rain to descend on you from heaven, to clean you therewith, to remove from you the stain of Satan, to strengthen your hearts, and to plant your feet firmly therewith.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Ith yughashsheekumu alnnuAAasa amanatan minhu wayunazzilu AAalaykum mina alssamai maan liyutahhirakum bihi wayuthhiba AAankum rijza alshshaytani waliyarbita AAala quloobikum wayuthabbita bihi alaqdama

12.arabic: إِذْ يُوحِى رَبُّكَ إِلَى ٱلْمَلَـٰٓئِكَةِ أَنِّى مَعَكُمْ فَثَبِّتُوا۟ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ ۚ سَأُلْقِى فِى قُلُوبِ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ ٱلرُّعْبَ فَٱضْرِبُوا۟ فَوْقَ ٱلْأَعْنَاقِ وَٱضْرِبُوا۟ مِنْهُمْ كُلَّ بَنَانٍ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: And the Lord said to the angels: "I am with you; go and strengthen the faithful. I shall fill the hearts of infidels with terror. So smite them on their necks and every joint, (and incapacitate them),"

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And when O dear Prophet, your Lord was inspiring the angels that, “I am with you – so make the believers stand firm; I will soon instil fear into the hearts of the disbelievers, so strike above the disbelievers’ necks and hit their each and every bone joint.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: When thy Lord was revealing to the angels, 'I am with you; so confirm the believers. I shall cast into the unbelievers' hearts terror; so smite above the necks, and smite every finger of them!'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And recall what time thy Lord inspired the angels: verily I am with you, so keep firm those who have believed; will cast horror into the hearts of those who have disbelieved, so smite them above the neck and smite of them every fingertip.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: (Remember) when your Lord inspired the angels, "Verily, I am with you, so keep firm those who have believed. I will cast terror into the hearts of those who have disbelieved, so strike them over the necks, and smite over all their fingers and toes."

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Your Lord inspired the angels: “I am with you, so support those who believe. I will cast terror into the hearts of those who disbelieve. So strike above the necks, and strike off every fingertip of theirs.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: And recall when your Lord inspired the angels: 'I am certainly with you. So make firm the feet of those who believe. I will cast terror into the hearts of those who disbelieve. So strike at their necks and strike at every pore and tip.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: (Remember) when your Lord revealed to the angels, "Verily, I am with you, so keep firm those who have believed. I will cast terror into the hearts of those who have disbelieved, so strike them over the necks, and smite over all their fingers and toes."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: When thy Lord inspired the angels, (saying): I am with you. So make those who believe stand firm. I will throw fear into the hearts of those who disbelieve. Then smite the necks and smite of them each finger.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Then your Lord signaled to the angels: ‘I am indeed with you; so steady the faithful. I will cast terror into the hearts of the faithless. So strike their necks, and strike their every limb joint!’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: And when Allah revealed to the angels, saying: 'I shall be with you. Give courage to the believers. I shall cast terror into the hearts of the unbelievers. Strike them above the necks, smite their finger tips'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: [Remember] when your Lord inspired to the angels, "I am with you, so strengthen those who have believed. I will cast terror into the hearts of those who disbelieved, so strike [them] upon the necks and strike from them every fingertip."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Your Lord inspired the angels saying, "I am with you. Encourage the believers. I shall cast terror into the hearts of the unbelievers and you will strike their heads and limbs;

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: When your Lord revealed to the angels: I am with you, therefore make firm those who believe. I will cast terror into the hearts of those who disbelieve. Therefore strike off their heads and strike off every fingertip of them.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: When your Lord commanded the angels, saying, "I am with you, so make those who believe stand firm. I will instill fear in the hearts of those who deny the truth: so strike their necks and strike all their finger joints!"

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Remember thy Lord inspired the angels (with the message): "I am with you: give firmness to the Believers: I will instil terror into the hearts of the Unbelievers: smite ye above their necks and smite all their finger-tips off them."

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Ith yoohee rabbuka ila almalaikati annee maAAakum fathabbitoo allatheena amanoo saolqee fee quloobi allatheena kafaroo alrruAAba faidriboo fawqa alaAAnaqi waidriboo minhum kulla bananin

13.arabic: ذَٰلِكَ بِأَنَّهُمْ شَآقُّوا۟ ٱللَّهَ وَرَسُولَهُۥ ۚ وَمَن يُشَاقِقِ ٱللَّهَ وَرَسُولَهُۥ فَإِنَّ ٱللَّهَ شَدِيدُ ٱلْعِقَابِ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: For they had opposed God and His Apostle; but whosoever opposes God and his Apostle (should know) that God is severe in retribution.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: This is because they opposed Allah and His Noble Messenger; and whoever opposes Allah and His Noble Messenger – then indeed Allah’s punishment is severe.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: That, because they had made a breach with God and with His Messenger; and whosoever makes a breach with God and with His Messenger, surely God is terrible in retribution.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: This, because they have resisted Allah and His apostle; and whosoever resisteth Allah and His apostle, then verily Allah severe in retribution.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: This is because they defied and disobeyed Allah and His Messenger. And whoever defies and disobeys Allah and His Messenger, then verily, Allah is Severe in punishment.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: That is because they opposed God and His Messenger. Whoever opposes God and His Messenger—God is severe in retribution.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: This is because they defied Allah and His Messenger. Whoever defies Allah and His Messenger must know that Allah is severe in punishment.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: This is because they defied and disobeyed Allah and His Messenger. And whoever defies and disobeys Allah and His Messenger, then verily, Allah is severe in punishment.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: That is because they opposed Allah and His messenger. Whoso opposeth Allah and His messenger, (for him) lo! Allah is severe in punishment.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: That, because they defied Allah and His Apostle. And whoever defies Allah and His Apostle, Allah is indeed severe in retribution.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: This is because they had made a breach with Allah and His Messenger. He who makes a breach with Allah and His Messenger indeed, Allah is stern in retribution.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: That is because they opposed Allah and His Messenger. And whoever opposes Allah and His Messenger - indeed, Allah is severe in penalty.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: they have opposed God and His Messengers." For those who oppose God and His Messengers, God has prepared a severe retribution.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: This is because they acted adversely to Allah and His Apostle; and whoever acts adversely to Allah and His Apostle-- then surely Allah is severe in requiting (evil).

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: That was because they defied God and His Messenger. He who defies God and His Messenger shall be severely punished by God.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: This because they contended against Allah and His Messenger: If any contend against Allah and His Messenger, Allah is strict in punishment.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Thalika biannahum shaqqoo Allaha warasoolahu waman yushaqiqi Allaha warasoolahu fainna Allaha shadeedu alAAiqabi

14.arabic: ذَٰلِكُمْ فَذُوقُوهُ وَأَنَّ لِلْكَـٰفِرِينَ عَذَابَ ٱلنَّارِ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: For you is this (punishment) to taste, for the infidels the torment of Hell.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Therefore taste this for now, and along with it for the disbelievers is the punishment of fire.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: That for you; therefore taste it; and that the chastisement of the Fire is for the unbelievers.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: This! taste it then, and know that for the infidels is the torment of the Fire.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: This is the torment, so taste it, and surely for the disbelievers is the torment of the Fire.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: “Here it is; so taste it.” For the disbelievers there is the suffering of the Fire.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: That is your punishment (from Allah). So taste this punishment, and know that for the unbelievers is the punishment of the Fire.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: This is (the torment), so taste it; and surely, for the disbelievers is the torment of the Fire.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: That (is the award), so taste it, and (know) that for disbelievers is the torment of the Fire.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: ‘Taste this, and [know] that for the faithless is the punishment of the Fire.’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: 'That for you, so taste it, the punishment of the Fire is for the unbelievers'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: "That [is yours], so taste it." And indeed for the disbelievers is the punishment of the Fire.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: We will say to them, "Endure the torment (as a consequence of your evil deeds); the unbelievers deserve nothing better than (the torment) of fire."

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: This-- taste it, and (know) that for the unbelievers is the chastisement of fire.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: That is your punishment, taste it then; and know that for those who deny the truth there is the punishment of the Fire.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Thus (will it be said): "Taste ye then of the (punishment): for those who resist Allah, is the penalty of the Fire."

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Thalikum fathooqoohu waanna lilkafireena AAathaba alnnari

15.arabic: يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوٓا۟ إِذَا لَقِيتُمُ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ زَحْفًا فَلَا تُوَلُّوهُمُ ٱلْأَدْبَارَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: O believers, when you meet unbelievers on the field of battle, do not turn your backs to them.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: O People who Believe! When you confront a large army of disbelievers in battle, do not turn your backs to them.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: O believers, when you encounter the unbelievers marching to battle, turn not your backs to them.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: O Ye who believe! whenever ye those who disbelieve matching meet slowly turn to them not your backs.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: O you who believe! When you meet those who disbelieve, in a battle-field, never turn your backs to them.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: O you who believe! When you meet those who disbelieve on the march, never turn your backs on them.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Believers, whenever you encounter a hostile force of unbelievers, do not turn your backs to them in flight.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: O you who believe! When you meet those who disbelieve, in a battlefield, never turn your backs to them.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: O ye who believe! When ye meet those who disbelieve in battle, turn not your backs to them.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: O you who have faith! When you encounter the faithless advancing [for battle], do not turn your backs [to flee] from them.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Believers, when you encounter unbelievers on the march do not turn your backs to them in flight.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: O you who have believed, when you meet those who disbelieve advancing [for battle], do not turn to them your backs [in flight].

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Believers, do not retreat when facing the marching army of the unbelievers, for no believer will turn back at that time except for strategic reasons or to join another band.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: O you who believe! when you meet those who disbelieve marching for war, then turn not your backs to them.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Believers, when you meet in battle those who deny the truth, never turn your backs on them:

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: O ye who believe! when ye meet the Unbelievers in hostile array, never turn your backs to them.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Ya ayyuha allatheena amanoo itha laqeetumu allatheena kafaroo zahfan fala tuwalloohumu aladbara

16.arabic: وَمَن يُوَلِّهِمْ يَوْمَئِذٍ دُبُرَهُۥٓ إِلَّا مُتَحَرِّفًا لِّقِتَالٍ أَوْ مُتَحَيِّزًا إِلَىٰ فِئَةٍ فَقَدْ بَآءَ بِغَضَبٍ مِّنَ ٱللَّهِ وَمَأْوَىٰهُ جَهَنَّمُ ۖ وَبِئْسَ ٱلْمَصِيرُ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: For any one who turns his back on that day, except to manoeuvre or rally to his side, will bring the wrath of God on himself, and have Hell as abode; and what an evil destination!

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And on that day whoever turns his back to them, except for a battle strategy or to join one’s own company, has then turned towards Allah’s wrath, and his destination is hell; and what an evil place of return!

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Whoso turns his back that day to them, unless withdrawing to fight again or removing to join another host, he is laden with the burden of God's anger, and his refuge is Gehenna -- an evil homecoming!

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And whosoever turneth his back to them on such a day, unless it be swerving to a fight or wriggling round to anot her company, hath surely drawn upon himself indignation from Allah, and his resort is Hell--an evil destination!

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And whoever turns his back to them on such a day - unless it be a stratagem of war, or to retreat to a troop (of his own), - he indeed has drawn upon himself wrath from Allah. And his abode is Hell, and worst indeed is that destination!

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Anyone who turns his back on them on that Day, except while maneuvering for battle, or to join another group, has incurred wrath from God, and his abode is Hell—what a miserable destination!

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: For he who turns his back on them on such ar occasion - except that it be for tactical reasons, or turning to join another company - he shall incur the wrath of Allah and Hell shall be his abode. It is an evil destination.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And whoever turns his back to them on such a day -- unless it be a stratagem of war, or to retreat to a troop (of his own) -- he indeed has drawn upon himself wrath from Allah. And his abode is Hell, and worst indeed is that destination!

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Whoso on that day turneth his back to them, unless manoeuvring for battle or intent to join a company, he truly hath incurred wrath from Allah, and his habitation will be hell, a hapless journey's end.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Whoever turns his back [to flee] from them that day—unless [he is] diverting to fight or retiring towards another troop—shall certainly earn Allah’s wrath, and his refuge shall be hell, an evil destination.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: If anyone on that day turns his back to them in flight, except when maneuvering to fight, or to join another party, he shall be laden with the Anger of Allah and Gehenna shall be his refuge an evil arrival.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And whoever turns his back to them on such a day, unless swerving [as a strategy] for war or joining [another] company, has certainly returned with anger [upon him] from Allah, and his refuge is Hell - and wretched is the destination.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: (Whoever deserts the believers) will incur the wrath of God and will dwell in hell, a terrible dwelling.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And whoever shall turn his back to them on that day-- unless he turn aside for the sake of fighting or withdraws to a company-- then he, indeed, becomes deserving of Allah's wrath, and his abode is hell; and an evil destination shall it be.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: whoever turns his back on such an occasion, unless it be as a stratagem of war, or in an endeavour to join another group [of the believers] will indeed draw down upon himself the wrath of God, and Hell shall be his abode and the worst indeed is that destination.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: If any do turn his back to them on such a day - unless it be in a stratagem of war, or to retreat to a troop (of his own)- he draws on himself the wrath of Allah, and his abode is Hell,- an evil refuge (indeed)!

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Waman yuwallihim yawmaithin duburahu illa mutaharrifan liqitalin aw mutahayyizan ila fiatin faqad baa bighadabin mina Allahi wamawahu jahannamu wabisa almaseeru

17.arabic: فَلَمْ تَقْتُلُوهُمْ وَلَـٰكِنَّ ٱللَّهَ قَتَلَهُمْ ۚ وَمَا رَمَيْتَ إِذْ رَمَيْتَ وَلَـٰكِنَّ ٱللَّهَ رَمَىٰ ۚ وَلِيُبْلِىَ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ مِنْهُ بَلَآءً حَسَنًا ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ سَمِيعٌ عَلِيمٌ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: It was not you who killed them, but God did so. You did not throw what you threw, (sand into the eyes of the enemy at Badr), but God, to bring out the best in the faithful by doing them a favour of His own. God is all-hearing and all-knowing.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: So you did not slay them, but in fact Allah slew them; and O dear Prophet (Mohammed – peace and blessings be upon him) you did not throw (the sand) when you did throw, but in fact Allah threw; and in order to bestow an excellent reward upon the Muslims; indeed Allah is the All Hearing, the All Knowing.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: You did not slay them, but God slew them; and when thou threwest, it was not thyself that threw, but God threw, and that He might confer on the believers a fair benefit; surely God is All-hearing, All-knowing.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Wherefore ye slew them not, but Allah slew them, and thou thrtwest not, When thou threWest, but Allah threw, in order that he might prove the believers with a goodly proving from Him. Verily Allah is Hearing, Knowing.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: You killed them not, but Allah killed them. And you (Muhammad SAW) threw not when you did throw but Allah threw, that He might test the believers by a fair trial from Him. Verily, Allah is All-Hearer, All-Knower.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: It was not you who killed them, but it was God who killed them. And it was not you who launched when you launched, but it was God who launched. That He may bestow upon the believers an excellent reward. God is Hearing and Knowing.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: So the fact is that it was not you, but it was Allah Who killed them; and it was not you when you threw [sand at them], but it was Allah Who threw it, (and the believers were employed for the task) that He might cause the believers to successfully pass through this test. Allah is All-Hearing, All-Knowing.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: You killed them not, but Allah killed them. And you threw not when you did throw, but Allah threw, that He might test the believers by a fair trial from Him. Verily, Allah is All-Hearer, All-Knower.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Ye (Muslims) slew them not, but Allah slew them. And thou (Muhammad) threwest not when thou didst throw, but Allah threw, that He might test the believers by a fair test from Him. Lo! Allah is Hearer, Knower.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: You did not kill them; rather, it was Allah who killed them; and you did not throw when you threw, rather, it was Allah who threw, that He might test the faithful with a good test from Himself. Indeed Allah is all-hearing, all-knowing.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: It was not you who killed them, but Allah slew them, neither was it you who threw at them. Allah threw at them in order that He confers on the believers a fair benefit. Indeed, Allah is Hearing, Knowing.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And you did not kill them, but it was Allah who killed them. And you threw not, [O Muhammad], when you threw, but it was Allah who threw that He might test the believers with a good test. Indeed, Allah is Hearing and Knowing.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: It was not you (believers) but God who slew the pagans. It was not you (Muhammad) but God who threw dust at them. He did this as a favorable test for the believers. God is All-hearing and All-knowing.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: So you did not slay them, but it was Allah Who slew them, and you did not smite when you smote (the enemy), but it was Allah Who smote, and that He might confer upon the believers a good gift from Himself; surely Allah is Hearing, Knowing.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: You did not kill them; it was God who killed them; and when you [Prophet] threw [sand] at them it was not you, but God who threw it so that He might confer on the believers a great favour from Himself. Surely, God is all hearing, all-knowing,

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: It is not ye who slew them; it was Allah: when thou threwest (a handful of dust), it was not thy act, but Allah's: in order that He might test the Believers by a gracious trial from Himself: for Allah is He Who heareth and knoweth (all things).

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Falam taqtuloohum walakinna Allaha qatalahum wama ramayta ith ramayta walakinna Allaha rama waliyubliya almumineena minhu balaan hasanan inna Allaha sameeAAun AAaleemun

18.arabic: ذَٰلِكُمْ وَأَنَّ ٱللَّهَ مُوهِنُ كَيْدِ ٱلْكَـٰفِرِينَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: That was that, but remember God will make the plots of the unbelievers contemptible.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Therefore take this, and (know that) Allah will weaken the scheme of the disbelievers.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: That for you; and that God weakens the unbelievers' guile.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Thus! And know that Allah weakenoth the contrivance of infidels.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: This (is the fact) and surely, Allah weakens the deceitful plots of the disbelievers.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Such is the case. God will undermine the strategy of the disbelievers.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: This is His manner of dealing with you. As for the unbelievers, Allah will surely undermine their designs.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: This (is the fact) and surely Allah weakens the deceitful plots of the disbelievers.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: That (is the case); and (know) that Allah (it is) Who maketh weak the plan of disbelievers.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Such is the case, and [know] that Allah undermines the stratagems of the faithless.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Allah will surely weaken the guile of the unbelievers.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: That [is so], and [also] that Allah will weaken the plot of the disbelievers.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: This is how God causes the (evil) plans of the unbelievers to fail.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: This, and that Allah is the weakener of the struggle of the unbelievers.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: that is what happened, and God will surely undermine the design of those who deny the truth.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: That, and also because Allah is He Who makes feeble the plans and stratagem of the Unbelievers.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Thalikum waanna Allaha moohinu kaydi alkafireena

19.arabic: إِن تَسْتَفْتِحُوا۟ فَقَدْ جَآءَكُمُ ٱلْفَتْحُ ۖ وَإِن تَنتَهُوا۟ فَهُوَ خَيْرٌ لَّكُمْ ۖ وَإِن تَعُودُوا۟ نَعُدْ وَلَن تُغْنِىَ عَنكُمْ فِئَتُكُمْ شَيْـًٔا وَلَوْ كَثُرَتْ وَأَنَّ ٱللَّهَ مَعَ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: You had asked for a judgement, so the judgement has come to you (In the form of victory for the faithful). So, if you desist it will be better for you. If you come back to it, We shall do the same, and your forces, however large, will not be of the least avail, for God is with those who believe.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: O disbelievers! If you seek a judgement, then this judgement has come to you; and if you desist, it is better for you; and if you return to mischief, We will punish you again; and your populace will not benefit you, however large it may be – and besides this, Allah is with the Muslims.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: If victory you are seeking, victory has already come upon you; and if you give over, it is better for you. But if you return, We shall return, and your host will avail you nothing though it be numerous; and that God is with the believers.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: If ye besought a judgement then surely a judgement hath come unto you. And if ye desist it Will be better for you; and if ye return. We will return; and your host shall avail you not, although numerous it be; and know that verily Allah is with the believers.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: (O disbelievers) if you ask for a judgement, now has the judgement come unto you and if you cease (to do wrong), it will be better for you, and if you return (to the attack), so shall We return, and your forces will be of no avail to you, however numerous it be, and verily, Allah is with the believers.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: If you desire a verdict, the verdict has come to you. And if you desist, it would be best for you. And if you return, We will return; and your troops, however numerous, will not benefit you. God is with the believers.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: (Tell the unbelievers:) 'If you have sought a judgement, then surely a judgement has come to you. And if you desist from disobedience, it is all the better for you. But if you revert to your mischief, We will again chastise you; and your host, howsoever numerous, will never be of any avail to you. Know well, Allah is with the believers.'

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: (O disbelievers) if you ask for a judgement, now has the judgement come unto you; and if you cease (to do wrong), it will be better for you, and if you return (to the attack), so shall We return, and your forces will be of no avail to you, however numerous they be; and verily, Allah is with the believers.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: (O Qureysh!) If ye sought a judgment, now hath the judgment come unto you. And if ye cease (from persecuting the believers) it will be better for you, but if ye return (to the attack) We also shall return. And your host will avail you naught, however numerous it be, and (know) that Allah is with the believers (in His Guidance).

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: If you sought a verdict, the verdict has certainly come to you; and if you cease [your belligerence against the Prophet and his followers], it is better for you, but if you resume, We [too] shall return and your troops will never avail you though they should be ever so many, and [know] that Allah is with the faithful.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: If you were seeking a victory, victory has now come to you. If you desist, it will be better for you. If you resume We will return, and even if your forces are numerous, they shall not help you at all. And Allah is with the believers.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: If you [disbelievers] seek the victory - the defeat has come to you. And if you desist [from hostilities], it is best for you; but if you return [to war], We will return, and never will you be availed by your [large] company at all, even if it should increase; and [that is] because Allah is with the believers.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: If you (the pagans) are looking for conquest, you certainly had a great chance (at the battle of Badr). But if you were to give up such a desire, it would be better for you. If you again wage war against Us, We will be ready for such a confrontation. Your man-power will be of no help to you no matter how much you have; God is with the believers.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: If you demanded a judgment, the judgment has then indeed come to you; and if you desist, it will be better for you; and if you turn back (to fight), We (too) shall turn back, and your forces shall avail you nothing, though they may be many, and (know) that Allah is with the believers.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: If you were seeking a judgement, a judgement has now come to you. If you desist, it will be the better for you. But if you return [to hostility] We too will return. And your host will avail you naught, however numerous it may be, and [know] that surely God is with the believers.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: (O Unbelievers!) if ye prayed for victory and judgment, now hath the judgment come to you: if ye desist (from wrong), it will be best for you: if ye return (to the attack), so shall We. Not the least good will your forces be to you even if they were multiplied: for verily Allah is with those who believe!

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: In tastaftihoo faqad jaakumu alfathu wain tantahoo fahuwa khayrun lakum wain taAAoodoo naAAud walan tughniya AAankum fiatukum shayan walaw kathurat waanna Allaha maAAa almumineena

20.arabic: يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوٓا۟ أَطِيعُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ وَرَسُولَهُۥ وَلَا تَوَلَّوْا۟ عَنْهُ وَأَنتُمْ تَسْمَعُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: O believers, obey God and His Messenger, and do not turn away from him when you hear (him speak);

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: O People who Believe! Obey Allah and His Noble Messenger, and do not turn away from him after you have heard him speak.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: O believers, obey God and His Messenger, and do not turn away from Him, even as you are listening;

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: O Ye who believe! obey Allah and His apostle and turn not away therefrom while ye hearken.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: O you who believe! Obey Allah and His Messenger, and turn not away from him (i.e. Messenger Muhammad SAW) while you are hearing.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: O you who believe! Obey God and His Messenger, and do not turn away from him when you hear.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Believers! Obey Allah and His Messenger and do not turn away from him after you hear his command.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: O you who believe! Obey Allah and His Messenger, and turn not away from him while you are hearing.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: O ye who believe! Obey Allah and His messenger, and turn not away from him when ye hear (him speak).

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: O you who have faith! Obey Allah and His Apostle, and do not turn away from him while you hear [him].

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Believers, obey Allah and His Messenger, and do not turn away from him when you are hearing.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: O you who have believed, obey Allah and His Messenger and do not turn from him while you hear [his order].

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Believers, obey God and His Messengers and do not turn away from Him when you hear (His commands).

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: O you who believe! obey Allah and His Apostle and do not turn back from Him while you hear.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Believers, obey God and His Messenger, and do not turn away from him now that you have heard all.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: O ye who believe! Obey Allah and His Messenger, and turn not away from him when ye hear (him speak).

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Ya ayyuha allatheena amanoo ateeAAoo Allaha warasoolahu wala tawallaw AAanhu waantum tasmaAAoona

21.arabic: وَلَا تَكُونُوا۟ كَٱلَّذِينَ قَالُوا۟ سَمِعْنَا وَهُمْ لَا يَسْمَعُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: And do not be like those who say: "We have heard," but do not hear.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And do not be like those who said, “We have heard”, whereas they do not hear.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: and be not as those who say, 'We hear,' and they hear not.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And be not like unto those who say: we hear, whereas they hearken not.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And be not like those who say: "We have heard," but they hear not.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And be not like those who say, “We hear,” when they do not hear.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: And do not be like those who say: 'We hear', though they do not hearken.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And be not like those who say: "We have heard," but they hear not.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Be not as those who say, we hear, and they hear not.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Do not be like those who say, ‘We hear,’ though they do not hear.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Do not be like those who say: 'We hear' but they do not listen.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And do not be like those who say, "We have heard," while they do not hear.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Do not be like those who said that they have heard (the Messenger's commands) but do not pay any attention to them.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And be not like those who said, We hear, and they did not obey.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Do not be like those who say, "We hear," but pay no heed to what they hear --

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Nor be like those who say, "We hear," but listen not:

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Wala takoonoo kaallatheena qaloo samiAAna wahum la yasmaAAoona

22.arabic: إِنَّ شَرَّ ٱلدَّوَآبِّ عِندَ ٱللَّهِ ٱلصُّمُّ ٱلْبُكْمُ ٱلَّذِينَ لَا يَعْقِلُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: The worst of creatures in the eyes of God are those who are deaf and dumb and devoid of sense.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Indeed the worst beasts in the sight of Allah are those (people) who are deaf, dumb – who do not have any sense.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Surely the worst of beasts in God's sight are those that are deaf and dumb and do not understand.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Verily the vilest of beasts with Allah are the deaf and dumb who understand not.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Verily! The worst of (moving) living creatures with Allah are the deaf and the dumb, those who understand not (i.e. the disbelievers).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: The worst of animals to God are the deaf and dumb—those who do not reason.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Indeed the worst kind of all beasts in the sight of Allah are the people that are deaf and dumb, and do not understand.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Verily, the worst of living creatures with Allah are the deaf and the dumb (the disbelievers), who understand not.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Lo! the worst of beasts in Allah's sight are the deaf, the dumb, who have no sense.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Indeed the worst of beasts in Allah’s sight are the deaf and dumb who do not exercise their reason.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: The worst beasts before Allah are those who are deaf, dumb, and do not understand.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Indeed, the worst of living creatures in the sight of Allah are the deaf and dumb who do not use reason.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: The most wicked beasts in the sight of God are the deaf and the dumb who have no understanding.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Surely the vilest of animals, in Allah's sight, are the deaf, the dumb, who do not understand.
