translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: the worst creatures in God's eyes are those who are deaf and dumb, and who possess no understanding.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: For the worst of beasts in the sight of Allah are the deaf and the dumb,- those who understand not.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Inna sharra alddawabbi AAinda Allahi alssummu albukmu allatheena la yaAAqiloona

23.arabic: وَلَوْ عَلِمَ ٱللَّهُ فِيهِمْ خَيْرًا لَّأَسْمَعَهُمْ ۖ وَلَوْ أَسْمَعَهُمْ لَتَوَلَّوا۟ وَّهُم مُّعْرِضُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: If God had seen any good in them He would surely have made them hear. Now even if He makes them hear they will turn away (in obduracy).

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And had Allah found any goodness in them, He would have made them hear; and had He made them hear they would, in the end, have turned away and gone back.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: If God had known of any good in them He would have made them hear; and if He had made them hear, they would have turned away, swerving aside.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And had Allah known in them any good He would surely have made them hearken; and even if He made them hear, they would surely turn away as backsliders.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Had Allah known of any good in them, He would indeed have made them listen, and even if He had made them listen, they would but have turned away, averse (to the truth).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Had God recognized any good in them, He would have made them hear; and had He made them hear, they would have turned away defiantly.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: And had Allah known in them any good He would surely, have made them hear; but (being as they, are) even if He made them hear, they would have surely turned away in aversion.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Had Allah known of any good in them, He would indeed have made them listen; and even if He had made them listen, they would but have turned away with aversion.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Had Allah known of any good in them He would have made them hear, but had He made them hear they would have turned away, averse.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Had Allah known any good in them, He would have surely made them hear, and were He to make them hear, surely they would turn away, being disregardful.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Indeed, had Allah known any good in them, He would have let them hear. But even if He had made them hear, they would have turned away, swerving aside.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Had Allah known any good in them, He would have made them hear. And if He had made them hear, they would [still] have turned away, while they were refusing.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Had they possessed any virtue, God would certainly have made them hear. Even if God were to make them hear, they would still turn away from (the words of God).

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And if Allah had known any good in them He would have made them hear, and if He makes them hear they would turn back while they withdraw.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: If God had found any good in them, He would certainly have made them hear; but being as they are, even if He makes them hear, they will turn away in aversion.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: If Allah had found in them any good. He would indeed have made them listen: (As it is), if He had made them listen, they would but have turned back and declined (Faith).

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Walaw AAalima Allahu feehim khayran laasmaAAahum walaw asmaAAahum latawallaw wahum muAAridoona

24.arabic: يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ ٱسْتَجِيبُوا۟ لِلَّهِ وَلِلرَّسُولِ إِذَا دَعَاكُمْ لِمَا يُحْيِيكُمْ ۖ وَٱعْلَمُوٓا۟ أَنَّ ٱللَّهَ يَحُولُ بَيْنَ ٱلْمَرْءِ وَقَلْبِهِۦ وَأَنَّهُۥٓ إِلَيْهِ تُحْشَرُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: O believers, respond to the call of God and His Prophet when he calls you to what will give you life (and preservation). Remember that God intervenes between man and his heart, and that you will be gathered before Him.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: O People who Believe! Present yourselves upon the command of Allah and His Noble Messenger, when the Noble Messenger calls you towards the matter that will bestow you life; and know that the command of Allah becomes a barrier between a man and his heart’s intentions, and that you will all be raised towards Him.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: O believers, respond to God and the Messenger when He calls you unto that which will give you life; and know that God stands between a man and his heart, and that to Him you shall be mustered.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: O Ye who believe! answer Allah and the apostle when he calleth you to that which quickeneth you, and know that verily Allah interposeth between a man and his heart, and that verily unto Him ye all shall be gathered.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: O you who believe! Answer Allah (by obeying Him) and (His) Messenger when he (SAW) calls you to that which will give you life, and know that Allah comes in between a person and his heart (i.e. He prevents an evil person to decide anything). And verily to Him you shall (all) be gathered.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: O you who believe! Respond to God and to the Messenger when He calls you to what will revive you. And know that God stands between a man and his heart, and that to Him you will be gathered.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Believers! Respond to Allah, and respond to the Messenger when he calls you to that which gives you life. Know well that Allah stands between a man and his heart, and it is to Him that all of you shall be mustered.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: O you who believe! Answer Allah and (His) Messenger when he (the Messenger) calls you to that which will give you life, and know that Allah comes between a person and his heart. And verily to Him you shall (all) be gathered.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: O ye who believe! Obey Allah, and the messenger when He calleth you to that which quickeneth you, and know that Allah cometh in between the man and his own heart, and that He it is unto Whom ye will be gathered.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: O you who have faith! Answer Allah and the Apostle when he summons you to that which will give you life. Know that Allah intervenes between a man and his heart and that toward Him you will be mustered.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Believers, respond to Allah and the Messenger when he calls you to that which revives you. Know that Allah is between the person and his heart, and that toHim you shall all be gathered.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: O you who have believed, respond to Allah and to the Messenger when he calls you to that which gives you life. And know that Allah intervenes between a man and his heart and that to Him you will be gathered.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Believers, listen to God and the Messengers when they call you to that which gives you life. Know that God is between a man and his heart and that before Him you will all be brought together.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: O you who believe! answer (the call of) Allah and His Apostle when he calls you to that which gives you life; and know that Allah intervenes between man and his heart, and that to Him you shall be gathered.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Believers, obey God and the Messenger when he calls you to that which gives you life. Know that God stands between man and his heart, and you shall all be gathered in His presence.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: O ye who believe! give your response to Allah and His Messenger, when He calleth you to that which will give you life; and know that Allah cometh in between a man and his heart, and that it is He to Whom ye shall (all) be gathered.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Ya ayyuha allatheena amanoo istajeeboo lillahi walilrrasooli itha daAAakum lima yuhyeekum waiAAlamoo anna Allaha yahoolu bayna almari waqalbihi waannahu ilayhi tuhsharoona

25.arabic: وَٱتَّقُوا۟ فِتْنَةً لَّا تُصِيبَنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا۟ مِنكُمْ خَآصَّةً ۖ وَٱعْلَمُوٓا۟ أَنَّ ٱللَّهَ شَدِيدُ ٱلْعِقَابِ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Beware of sedition, which does not affect the oppressors alone among you, and know that the punishment of God is severe.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And fear the turmoil which will certainly not fall only upon a few selected unjust people among you; and know that Allah’s punishment is severe.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: And fear a trial which shall surely not smite in particular the evildoers among you; and know that God is terrible in retribution.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And fear the trial that shall not afflict those alone who among you do Wrong; and know that verily Allah severe in chastising.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And fear the Fitnah (affliction and trial, etc.) which affects not in particular (only) those of you who do wrong (but it may afflict all the good and the bad people), and know that Allah is Severe in punishment.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And beware of discord which does not afflict the wrongdoers among you exclusively; and know that God is severe in retribution.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: And guard against the mischief that will not only bring punishment to the wrong-doers among you. Know well that Allah is severe in punishment.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And fear the Fitnah (affliction and trial) which affects not in particular (only) those of you who do wrong, and know that Allah is severe in punishment.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And guard yourselves against a chastisement which cannot fall exclusively on those of you who are wrong-doers, and know that Allah is severe in punishment.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: And beware of a punishment, which shall not visit the wrongdoers among you exclusively, and know that Allah is severe in retribution.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: And be cautious against a sedition that will not smite the harmdoers alone. Know that the punishment of Allah is Stern.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And fear a trial which will not strike those who have wronged among you exclusively, and know that Allah is severe in penalty.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Guard yourselves against discord among yourselves so that it will not mislead anyone of you, especially the unjust, and know that God's retribution is most severe.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And fear an affliction which may not smite those of you in particular who are unjust; and know that Allah is severe in requiting (evil).

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: And beware of an affliction that will not smite exclusively those among you who have done wrong. Know that God is severe in exacting retribution.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: And fear tumult or oppression, which affecteth not in particular (only) those of you who do wrong: and know that Allah is strict in punishment.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Waittaqoo fitnatan la tuseebanna allatheena thalamoo minkum khassatan waiAAlamoo anna Allaha shadeedu alAAiqabi

26.arabic: وَٱذْكُرُوٓا۟ إِذْ أَنتُمْ قَلِيلٌ مُّسْتَضْعَفُونَ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ تَخَافُونَ أَن يَتَخَطَّفَكُمُ ٱلنَّاسُ فَـَٔاوَىٰكُمْ وَأَيَّدَكُم بِنَصْرِهِۦ وَرَزَقَكُم مِّنَ ٱلطَّيِّبَـٰتِ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَشْكُرُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Remember, when you were few and powerless in the land, afraid of despoliation at the hands of men. But then God sheltered and helped you to strength, and provided for you good things that you may perhaps be grateful.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And remember when you were only a few and meek in the land, and feared that men may snatch you away – He therefore gave you refuge and strengthened you with His help, and gave you good things as sustenance, so that you may be thankful.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: And remember when you were few and abased in the land, and were fearful that the people would snatch you away; but He gave you refuge, and confirmed you with His help, and provided you with the good things, that haply you might be thankful.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And remember what time ye were few and downtrodden in the land fearing that the people would snatch you away; then He gave you refuge and strengthened you with His succour, and provided you with good things that haply ye might return thanks.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And remember when you were few and were reckoned weak in the land, and were afraid that men might kidnap you, but He provided a safe place for you, strengthened you with His Help, and provided you with good things so that you might be grateful.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And remember when you were few, oppressed in the land, fearing that people may capture you; but He sheltered you, and supported you with His victory, and provided you with good things—so that you may be thankful.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: And recall when you were few in numbers and deemed weak in the land, fearful lest people do away with you. Then He provided you refuge, strengthened you with His help, and provided you sustenance with good things that you may be grateful.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And remember when you were few and were reckoned weak in the land, and were afraid that men might kidnap you, but He provided a safe place for you, strengthened you with His help, and provided you with good things (for livelihood) so that you might be grateful.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And remember, when ye were few and reckoned feeble in the land, and were in fear lest men should extirpate you, how He gave you refuge, and strengthened you with His help, and made provision of good things for you, that haply ye might be thankful.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Remember when you were few, abased in the land, and feared lest the people should despoil you, and He gave you refuge, and strengthened you with His help, and provided you with all the good things so that you may give thanks.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: And remember how He gave you shelter when you were few in number and considered weak in the land, ever fearing that the people would snatch you away; but He gave you refuge and supported you with His victory and provided you with goodness, so that you might give thanks.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And remember when you were few and oppressed in the land, fearing that people might abduct you, but He sheltered you, supported you with His victory, and provided you with good things - that you might be grateful.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Recall the time when you (the believers) were only a few suppressed people in the land, afraid of being terrorized by the people. God gave you shelter, supported you with His help, and bestowed on you pure provisions so that perhaps you would give Him thanks.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And remember when you were few, deemed weak in the land, fearing lest people might carry you off by force, but He sheltered you and strengthened you with His aid and gave you of the good things that you may give thanks.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Remember when you were few in number and were accounted weak in the land, ever fearing the onslaught of your enemies, but He provided you with shelter, and supported you with His help and provided you with good things, so that you might be grateful.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Call to mind when ye were a small (band), despised through the land, and afraid that men might despoil and kidnap you; But He provided a safe asylum for you, strengthened you with His aid, and gave you Good things for sustenance: that ye might be grateful.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Waothkuroo ith antum qaleelun mustadAAafoona fee alardi takhafoona an yatakhattafakumu alnnasu faawakum waayyadakum binasrihi warazaqakum mina alttayyibati laAAallakum tashkuroona

27.arabic: يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ لَا تَخُونُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ وَٱلرَّسُولَ وَتَخُونُوٓا۟ أَمَـٰنَـٰتِكُمْ وَأَنتُمْ تَعْلَمُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: O you who believe, do not be faithless to God and His Apostle, nor violate your trusts knowingly.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: O People who Believe! Do not betray Allah and His Noble Messenger, nor purposely defraud your trusts.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: O believers, betray not God and the Messenger, and betray not your trusts and that wittingly;

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: O Ye who believe! defraud not Allah and the apostle, nor defraud your trusts while ye know.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: O you who believe! Betray not Allah and His Messenger, nor betray knowingly your Amanat (things entrusted to you, and all the duties which Allah has ordained for you).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: O you who believe! Do not betray God and the Messenger, nor betray your trusts, while you know.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Believers! Do not be unfaithful to Allah and the Messenger, nor be knowingly unfaithful to your trusts.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: O you who believe! Betray not Allah and His Messenger, nor betray knowingly your Amanat (things entrusted to you).

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: O ye who believe! Betray not Allah and His messenger, nor knowingly betray your trusts.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: O you who have faith! Do not betray Allah and the Apostle, and do not betray your trusts knowingly.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Believers, do not betray Allah and the Messenger, nor knowingly betray your trust.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: O you who have believed, do not betray Allah and the Messenger or betray your trusts while you know [the consequence].

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Believers, do not be dishonest to God and the Messengers or knowingly abuse your trust.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: O you who believe! be not unfaithful to Allah and the Apostle, nor be unfaithful to your trusts while you know.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Do not betray God and His Messenger, and do not knowingly violate your trusts.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: O ye that believe! betray not the trust of Allah and the Messenger, nor misappropriate knowingly things entrusted to you.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Ya ayyuha allatheena amanoo la takhoonoo Allaha waalrrasoola watakhoonoo amanatikum waantum taAAlamoona

28.arabic: وَٱعْلَمُوٓا۟ أَنَّمَآ أَمْوَٰلُكُمْ وَأَوْلَـٰدُكُمْ فِتْنَةٌ وَأَنَّ ٱللَّهَ عِندَهُۥٓ أَجْرٌ عَظِيمٌ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Know that your worldly possessions and your children are just a temptation, and that God has greater rewards with Him.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And know that your wealth and your children are a test, and that with Allah is an immense reward.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: and know that your wealth and your children are a trial, and that with God is a mighty wage.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And know that your riches and your children are but a temptation, and that verily Allah: with Him is a mighty hire.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And know that your possessions and your children are but a trial and that surely with Allah is a mighty reward.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And know that your possessions and your children are a test, and that God possesses an immense reward.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Know well that your belongings and your children are but a trial, and that with Allah there is a mighty reward.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And know that your possessions and your children are but a trial and that surely, with Allah is a mighty reward.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And know that your possessions and your children are a test, and that with Allah is immense reward.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Know that your possessions and children are only a test, and that Allah—with Him is a great reward.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Know that your children and your wealth are but a temptation, and that the reward with Allah is great.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And know that your properties and your children are but a trial and that Allah has with Him a great reward.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Know that your possessions and children are a temptation for you and that God has the greatest reward (for the righteous ones).

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And know that your property and your children are a temptation, and that Allah is He with Whom there is a mighty reward.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Know that your wealth and children are a trial and that there is an immense reward with God.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: And know ye that your possessions and your progeny are but a trial; and that it is Allah with Whom lies your highest reward.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: WaiAAlamoo annama amwalukum waawladukum fitnatun waanna Allaha AAindahu ajrun AAatheemun

29.arabic: يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوٓا۟ إِن تَتَّقُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ يَجْعَل لَّكُمْ فُرْقَانًا وَيُكَفِّرْ عَنكُمْ سَيِّـَٔاتِكُمْ وَيَغْفِرْ لَكُمْ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ ذُو ٱلْفَضْلِ ٱلْعَظِيمِ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: O believers, if you follow the path shown by God, He will give you a standard (of right and wrong), and overlook your sins, and forgive you. God is abounding in benevolence.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: O People who Believe! If you fear Allah, He will bestow upon you (the criterion) that with which you will separate the truth from falsehood, and He will unburden your misdeeds and forgive you; and Allah is the Extremely Munificent.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: O believers, if you fear God, He will assign you a salvation, and acquit you of your evil deeds, and forgive you; and God is of bounty abounding.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: O Ye who believe! if ye fear Allah He will make for you a distinction and will expiate for you your misdeeds, and forgive you; and Allah is Owner of Mighty Grace.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: O you who believe! If you obey and fear Allah, He will grant you Furqan a criterion [(to judge between right and wrong), or (Makhraj, i.e. making a way for you to get out from every difficulty)], and will expiate for you your sins, and forgive you, and Allah is the Owner of the Great Bounty.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: O you who believe! If you remain conscious of God, He will give you a criterion, and will remit from you your sins, and will forgive you. God is possessor of infinite grace.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Believers! If you fear Allah He will grant you a criterion and will cleanse you of your sins and forgive you. Allah is Lord of abounding bounty.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: O you who believe! If you obey and fear Allah, He will grant you Furqan, and will expiate for you your sins, and forgive you; and Allah is the Owner of the great bounty.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: O ye who believe! If ye keep your duty to Allah, He will give you discrimination (between right and wrong) and will rid you of your evil thoughts and deeds, and will forgive you. Allah is of Infinite Bounty.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: O you who have faith! If you are wary of Allah, He shall appoint a criterion for you, and absolve you of your misdeeds, and forgive you, for Allah is dispenser of a great grace.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Believers, if you fear Allah, He will give you a criteria and cleanse you of your sins and forgive you. Allah is the Owner of great bounty.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: O you who have believed, if you fear Allah, He will grant you a criterion and will remove from you your misdeeds and forgive you. And Allah is the possessor of great bounty.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Believers, if you fear God, He will give you guidance, will expiate your bad deeds and forgive you. God's favors are the greatest.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: O you who believe! If you are careful of (your duty to) Allah, He will grant you a distinction and do away with your evils and forgive you; and Allah is the Lord of mighty grace.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Believers, if you fear God, He will grant you the ability to discriminate between right and wrong, and will forgive you your sins: for God is limitless in His great bounty.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: O ye who believe! if ye fear Allah, He will grant you a criterion (to judge between right and wrong), remove from you (all) evil (that may afflict) you, and forgive you: for Allah is the Lord of grace unbounded.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Ya ayyuha allatheena amanoo in tattaqoo Allaha yajAAal lakum furqanan wayukaffir AAankum sayyiatikum wayaghfir lakum waAllahu thoo alfadli alAAatheemi

30.arabic: وَإِذْ يَمْكُرُ بِكَ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ لِيُثْبِتُوكَ أَوْ يَقْتُلُوكَ أَوْ يُخْرِجُوكَ ۚ وَيَمْكُرُونَ وَيَمْكُرُ ٱللَّهُ ۖ وَٱللَّهُ خَيْرُ ٱلْمَـٰكِرِينَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Remember, when the infidels contrived to make you a prisoner or to murder or expel you, they plotted, but God also planned; and God's plan is the best.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And remember O dear Prophet when the disbelievers were scheming against you to either imprison you, or to kill you or to banish you; and they were scheming, and Allah was making His secret plan; and Allah’s secret plan is the best.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: And when the unbelievers were devising against thee, to confine thee, or slay thee, or to expel thee, and were devising, and God was devising; and God is the best of devisers.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And recall what time those who disbelieved were plotting against thee to confine thee or to slay thee or to drive thee forth: they were plotting and Allah was plotting, and Allah is the Best of plotters.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And (remember) when the disbelievers plotted against you (O Muhammad SAW) to imprison you, or to kill you, or to get you out (from your home, i.e. Makkah); they were plotting and Allah too was planning, and Allah is the Best of the planners.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: When the disbelievers plotted against you, to imprison you, or kill you, or expel you. They planned, and God planned, but God is the Best of planners.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: And recall how those who disbelieved schemed against you to take you captive, or kill you, or drive you away. They schemed and Allah did also scheme. Allah is the best of those who scheme.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And (remember) when the disbelievers plotted against you to imprison you, or to kill you, or to expel you (from your home, Makkah); they were plotting and Allah too was plotting; and Allah is the best of plotters.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And when those who disbelieve plot against thee (O Muhammad) to wound thee fatally, or to kill thee or to drive thee forth; they plot, but Allah (also) plotteth; and Allah is the best of plotters.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: When the faithless plotted against you to take you captive, or to kill or expel you—they plotted and Allah devised, and Allah is the best of devisers.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: And when the unbelievers plotted against you (Prophet Muhammad). They sought to either take you captive or have you killed, or expelled. They plotted but Allah (in reply) also plotted. Allah is the Best in plotting.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And [remember, O Muhammad], when those who disbelieved plotted against you to restrain you or kill you or evict you [from Makkah]. But they plan, and Allah plans. And Allah is the best of planners.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: The unbelievers planned to imprison, murder or expel you (Muhammad) from your city. They make evil plans but God too plans and God's plans are the best.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And when those who disbelieved devised plans against you that they might confine you or slay you or drive you away; and they devised plans and Allah too had arranged a plan; and Allah is the best of planners.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Remember how those who bent on denying the truth plotted against you to imprison you or kill you or expel you: they schemed -- but God also schemed. God is the best of schemers.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Remember how the Unbelievers plotted against thee, to keep thee in bonds, or slay thee, or get thee out (of thy home). They plot and plan, and Allah too plans; but the best of planners is Allah.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Waith yamkuru bika allatheena kafaroo liyuthbitooka aw yaqtulooka aw yukhrijooka wayamkuroona wayamkuru Allahu waAllahu khayru almakireena

31.arabic: وَإِذَا تُتْلَىٰ عَلَيْهِمْ ءَايَـٰتُنَا قَالُوا۟ قَدْ سَمِعْنَا لَوْ نَشَآءُ لَقُلْنَا مِثْلَ هَـٰذَآ ۙ إِنْ هَـٰذَآ إِلَّآ أَسَـٰطِيرُ ٱلْأَوَّلِينَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: When Our messages were read out to them, they said: "We have heard. We could certainly compose (writings) like them if we choose. They are but only tales of long ago."

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And when Our verses are recited to them they say, “Yes, we have heard – if we wanted we could also say something like this – these are nothing but stories of former people!”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: And When Our signs were being recited to them, they said, 'We have already heard; if we wished, we could say the like of this; this is naught but the fairy-tales of the ancients.'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And when Our revelations are rehearsed unto them, they say: we have heard; we could if we willed, surely say something the like thereof; naught is this but fables of the ancients.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And when Our Verses (of the Quran) are recited to them, they say: "We have heard this (the Quran); if we wish we can say the like of this. This is nothing but the tales of the ancients."

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And when Our revelations are recited to them, they say, “We have heard. Had we wanted, we could have said the like of this; these are nothing but myths of the ancients.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: And when Our verses are recited to them, they say: 'We have heard. We could, if we willed, compose the like of it. They, are nothing but fables of the ancient times.'

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And when Our Ayat are recited to them, they say: "We have heard (the Qur'an); if we wish we can say the like of this. This is nothing but the tales of the ancients."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And when Our revelations are recited unto them they say: We have heard. If we wish we can speak the like of this. Lo! this is naught but fables of the men of old.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: When Our signs are recited to them, they say, ‘We have heard already. If we want, we [too] can say like this. These are nothing but myths of the ancients.’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Whenever Our verses are recited to them, they say: 'We have heard them, if we wished, we could speak its like. They are but tales of the ancients'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And when Our verses are recited to them, they say, "We have heard. If we willed, we could say [something] like this. This is not but legends of the former peoples."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: When Our revelations are recited to them (the unbelievers), they say, "We have heard them. Had we wanted, we could also have composed such statements; they are no more than ancient legends."

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And when Our communications are recited to them, they say: We have heard indeed; if we pleased we could say the like of it; this is nothing but the stories of the ancients.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Whenever Our revelations are recited to them, they say, "We have heard them. If we wished, we could produce the like. They are nothing but the fables of the ancients."

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: When Our Signs are rehearsed to them, they say: "We have heard this (before): if we wished, we could say (words) like these: these are nothing but tales of the ancients."

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Waitha tutla AAalayhim ayatuna qaloo qad samiAAna law nashao laqulna mithla hatha in hatha illa asateeru alawwaleena

32.arabic: وَإِذْ قَالُوا۟ ٱللَّهُمَّ إِن كَانَ هَـٰذَا هُوَ ٱلْحَقَّ مِنْ عِندِكَ فَأَمْطِرْ عَلَيْنَا حِجَارَةً مِّنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ أَوِ ٱئْتِنَا بِعَذَابٍ أَلِيمٍ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: They had also said: "If this be the truth from you, O God, then rain down on us stones from the skies, or inflict a grievous punishment upon us."

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And when they said, “O Allah! If this (the Qur’an) is really the truth from You, then shower upon us a rain of stones from the sky, or bring upon us some painful punishment.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: And when they said, 'O God, if this be indeed the truth from Thee, then rain down upon us stones out of heaven, or bring us a painful chastisement.'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And recall what time they said: O God! if this indeed be the truth from before Thee, then rain down stones upon us from heaven or bring on us a torment afflictive.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And (remember) when they said: "O Allah! If this (the Quran) is indeed the truth (revealed) from You, then rain down stones on us from the sky or bring on us a painful torment."

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And they said, “Our God, if this is the truth from You, then rain down on us stones from the sky, or visit us with a painful affliction.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: And also recall when they said: 'O Allah! If this indeed be the truth from You, then rain down stones upon us from heaven, or bring upon us a painful chastisement.'

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And (remember) when they said: "O Allah! If this (the Qur'an) is indeed the truth (revealed) from You, then rain down stones on us from the sky or bring on us a painful torment."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And when they said: O Allah! If this be indeed the truth from Thee, then rain down stones on us or bring on us some painful doom!

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: And when they said, ‘O Allah, if this be the truth from You, rain down upon us stones from the sky, or bring us a painful punishment.’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: When they say: 'O Allah, if this is indeed the truth from You, rain down upon us stones from heaven or bring us a painful punishment.'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And [remember] when they said, "O Allah, if this should be the truth from You, then rain down upon us stones from the sky or bring us a painful punishment."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: They also say, "Lord, if this (Quran) is the Truth from you, shower down stones on us from the sky instead of rain or send us a painful punishment".

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And when they said: O Allah! if this is the truth from Thee, then rain upon us stones from heaven or inflict on us a painful punishment.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: They also said, "God, if this really is the truth from You, then rain down upon us stones from heaven, or send us some other painful punishment."

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Remember how they said: "O Allah if this is indeed the Truth from Thee, rain down on us a shower of stones form the sky, or send us a grievous penalty."

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Waith qaloo allahumma in kana hatha huwa alhaqqa min AAindika faamtir AAalayna hijaratan mina alssamai awi itina biAAathabin aleemin

33.arabic: وَمَا كَانَ ٱللَّهُ لِيُعَذِّبَهُمْ وَأَنتَ فِيهِمْ ۚ وَمَا كَانَ ٱللَّهُ مُعَذِّبَهُمْ وَهُمْ يَسْتَغْفِرُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: But God would not choose to punish them while you are in their midst, nor afflict them when they are seeking forgiveness.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And it is not for Allah to punish them while you O dear Prophet (Mohammed – peace and blessings be upon him) are amongst them; and Allah will not punish them as long as they are seeking forgiveness. (Prophet Mohammed – peace and blessings be upon him – is a mercy unto mankind.)

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: But God would never chastise them, with thee among them; God would never chastise them as they begged forgiveness.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And Allah is not one to chastise them whilst thou art in their midst, nor was Allah going to chastise them while they were asking forgiveness.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And Allah would not punish them while you (Muhammad SAW) are amongst them, nor will He punish them while they seek (Allah's) Forgiveness.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: But God would not punish them while you are amongst them. And God would not punish them as long as they seek forgiveness.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: But Allah was not to chastise them while you are in their midst; nor was Allah going to chastise them while they sought His forgiveness.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And Allah would not punish them while you (Muhammad) are among them, nor will He punish them while they seek (Allah's) forgiveness.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: But Allah would not punish them while thou wast with them, nor will He punish them while they seek forgiveness.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: But Allah will not punish them while you are in their midst, nor will Allah punish them while they plead for forgiveness.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: But Allah was not to punish them whilst you were living in their midst. Nor would Allah punish them if they repeatedly ask forgiveness of Him.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: But Allah would not punish them while you, [O Muhammad], are among them, and Allah would not punish them while they seek forgiveness.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: God would not punish them while you were among them nor while they were asking for forgiveness.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: But Allah was not going to chastise them while you were among them, nor is Allah going to chastise them while yet they ask for forgiveness.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: But God would not punish them while you [Prophet] were in their midst, nor would He punish them so long as they sought forgiveness.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: But Allah was not going to send them a penalty whilst thou wast amongst them; nor was He going to send it whilst they could ask for pardon.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Wama kana Allahu liyuAAaththibahum waanta feehim wama kana Allahu muAAaththibahum wahum yastaghfiroona

34.arabic: وَمَا لَهُمْ أَلَّا يُعَذِّبَهُمُ ٱللَّهُ وَهُمْ يَصُدُّونَ عَنِ ٱلْمَسْجِدِ ٱلْحَرَامِ وَمَا كَانُوٓا۟ أَوْلِيَآءَهُۥٓ ۚ إِنْ أَوْلِيَآؤُهُۥٓ إِلَّا ٱلْمُتَّقُونَ وَلَـٰكِنَّ أَكْثَرَهُمْ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: But what is there so special they have that God should not punish them when they obstruct people from the Holy Mosque, though they are not its (appointed) guardians? Its guardians could be only those who are pious and devout. But most of them do not know.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And what is with them that Allah should not punish them, whereas in fact they prevent from the Sacred Mosque and they are not worthy (of being the custodians) of it; only the pious are its befitting custodians, but most of them do not have knowledge.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: But what have they now, that God should not chastise them, when they are barring from the Holy Mosque, not being its protectors? Its only protectors are the godfearing; but most of them know not.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And what aileth them that Allah should not chastise them when they are hindering people from the Sacred Mosque, whereas they are not even the keepers thereof - its keepers are none but the God-fearing-- but most of them know not.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And why should not Allah punish them while they stop (men) from Al-Masjid-al-Haram, and they are not its guardians? None can be its guardian except Al-Muttaqun (the pious - see V. 2:2), but most of them know not.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Yet why should God not punish them, when they are turning others away from the Sacred Mosque, although they are not its custodians? Its rightful custodians are the pious; but most of them do not know.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: But what prevents Allah from chastising them now when they are hindering people from the Holy Mosque, even though they are not even its true guardians. For its true guardians are none but the God-fearing, though most of them do not know that.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And why should not Allah punish them while they hinder (men) from Al-Masjid Al-Haram, and they are not its guardians None can be its guardians except those who have Taqwa, but most of them know not.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: What (plea) have they that Allah should not punish them, when they debar (His servants) from the Inviolable Place of Worship, though they are not its fitting guardians. Its fitting guardians are those only who keep their duty to Allah. But most of them know not.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: What [excuse] have they that Allah should not punish them, when they bar [the faithful] from the Holy Mosque, and they are not its custodians? Its custodians are only the Godwary, but most of them do not know.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: And why should Allah not punish them, when they have barred others from the Sacred Mosque, although they were not its guardians? Its only guardians are the cautious, though most of them do not know.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: But why should Allah not punish them while they obstruct [people] from al-Masjid al- Haram and they were not [fit to be] its guardians? Its [true] guardians are not but the righteous, but most of them do not know.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Why should God not punish them when they hinder people from entering the sacred mosque? They are not its true patrons. Only the righteous ones are its patrons, but most of the pagans do not know.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And what (excuse) have they that Allah should not chastise them while they hinder (men) from the Sacred Mosque and they are not (fit to be) guardians of it; its guardians are only those who guard (against evil), but most of them do not know.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Yet why should God not punish them when they debar people from the Sacred Mosque, although they are not its guardians? Its rightful guardians are those who fear God, though most of them do not realize it.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: But what plea have they that Allah should not punish them, when they keep out (men) from the sacred Mosque - and they are not its guardians? No men can be its guardians except the righteous; but most of them do not understand.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Wama lahum alla yuAAaththibahumu Allahu wahum yasuddoona AAani almasjidi alharami wama kanoo awliyaahu in awliyaohu illa almuttaqoona walakinna aktharahum la yaAAlamoona

35.arabic: وَمَا كَانَ صَلَاتُهُمْ عِندَ ٱلْبَيْتِ إِلَّا مُكَآءً وَتَصْدِيَةً ۚ فَذُوقُوا۟ ٱلْعَذَابَ بِمَا كُنتُمْ تَكْفُرُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Their worship in the House of God has been no more than whistling and clapping. So they have to taste the punishment for disbelief.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And their prayer near the Kaa’bah is nothing except whistling and clapping; “So now taste the punishment – the result of your disbelief.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: And their prayer at the House is nothing but a whistling and a clapping of hands -- therefore taste you now the chastisement for your unbelief!

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And naught was their prayer at the House but whistling and hand-clapping. Taste then the torment for ye were to disbelieve.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Their Salat (prayer) at the House (of Allah, i.e. the Ka'bah at Makkah) was nothing but whistling and clapping of hands. Therefore taste the punishment because you used to disbelieve.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Their prayer at the House was nothing but whistling and clapping—so taste the punishment for your blasphemy.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Their Prayer at the House is nothing but whistling and handclapping. Taste, then, this chastisement for your denying the truth.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Their Salah at the House was nothing but Muka' and Tasdiyah. Therefore taste the punishment because you used to disbelieve.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And their worship at the (holy) House is naught but whistling and hand-clapping. Therefore (it is said unto them): Taste of the doom because ye disbelieve.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Their prayer at the House is nothing but whistling and clapping. So taste the punishment because of what you used to defy.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Their prayers at the Sacred House are nothing but whistling and clapping. Therefore, taste the punishment for your disbelief.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And their prayer at the House was not except whistling and handclapping. So taste the punishment for what you disbelieved.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Their (unbelievers) prayer at the mosque is nothing but whistling and clapping of hands. We shall tell them, "Suffer torment for your disbelief."

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And their prayer before the House is nothing but whistling and clapping of hands; taste then the chastisement, for you disbelieved.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Their prayers at the Sacred House are nothing but whistling and clapping of hands. "So taste the punishment because of your denial."

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Their prayer at the House (of Allah) is nothing but whistling and clapping of hands: (Its only answer can be), "Taste ye the penalty because ye blasphemed."

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Wama kana salatuhum AAinda albayti illa mukaan watasdiyatan fathooqoo alAAathaba bima kuntum takfuroona

36.arabic: إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ يُنفِقُونَ أَمْوَٰلَهُمْ لِيَصُدُّوا۟ عَن سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ ۚ فَسَيُنفِقُونَهَا ثُمَّ تَكُونُ عَلَيْهِمْ حَسْرَةً ثُمَّ يُغْلَبُونَ ۗ وَٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوٓا۟ إِلَىٰ جَهَنَّمَ يُحْشَرُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Those who disbelieve spend their possessions on turning men away from God. They will go on spending and rue it in the end, and will be subdued. But those who remain disbelievers shall be gathered into Hell

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Indeed the disbelievers spend their wealth in order to prevent people from the way of Allah; so they will spend it now, and then regret over it, then they will be defeated; and the disbelievers will be gathered towards hell.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: The unbelievers expend their wealth to bar from God's way, and still they will expend it, till it is an anguish for them, then be overthrown, and the unbelievers will be mustered into Gehenna,

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Verily those who disbelieve are expending their riches in order to hinder People from the way of Allah; so they will go on expending them; thereafter they shall become an anguish unto them; then they shall be overcome. And those who disbelieve shall be gathered for Hell.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Verily, those who disbelieve spend their wealth to hinder (men) from the Path of Allah, and so will they continue to spend it; but in the end it will become an anguish for them. Then they will be overcomed. And those who disbelieve will be gathered unto Hell.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Those who disbelieve spend their wealth to repel from God’s path. They will spend it, then it will become a source of sorrow for them, and then they will be defeated. Those who disbelieve will be herded into Hell.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Surely those who deny the truth spend their wealth to hinder people from the way of Allah, and will continue to so spend until their efforts become a source of intense regret for them, and then they will be vanquished, and then these deniers of the truth will be driven to Hell,

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Verily, those who disbelieve spend their wealth to hinder (men) from the path of Allah, and so will they continue to spend it; but in the end it will become an anguish for them. Then they will be overcome. And those who disbelieve will be gathered unto Hell.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Lo! those who disbelieve spend their wealth in order that they may debar (men) from the way of Allah. They will spend it, then it will become an anguish for them, then they will be conquered. And those who disbelieve will be gathered unto hell,

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Indeed the faithless spend their wealth to bar from the way of Allah. Soon they will have spent it, then it will be a cause of regret to them, then they will be overcome, and the faithless will be gathered toward Hell,

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Those who disbelieve spend their wealth to bar (others) from the Path of Allah. They will spend it; but it will become a regret for them, then they will be overcome. The unbelievers shall be gathered in Gehenna,

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Indeed, those who disbelieve spend their wealth to avert [people] from the way of Allah. So they will spend it; then it will be for them a [source of] regret; then they will be overcome. And those who have disbelieved - unto Hell they will be gathered.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: The disbelievers spend their wealth to turn men away from the way of God. They will continue to spend but it will become a source of regret for them and they will be defeated (because of their evil plans). The disbelievers will be gathered all togdher in Hell.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Surely those who disbelieve spend their wealth to hinder (people) from the way of Allah; so they shall spend it, then it shall be to them an intense regret, then they shall be overcome; and those who disbelieve shall be driven together to hell.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Those who are bent on denying the truth are spending their wealth in debarring others from the path of God. They will continue to spend it in this way till, in the end, this spending will become a source of intense regret for them, and then they will be overcome. And those who denied the truth will be gathered together in Hell.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: The Unbelievers spend their wealth to hinder (man) from the path of Allah, and so will they continue to spend; but in the end they will have (only) regrets and sighs; at length they will be overcome: and the Unbelievers will be gathered together to Hell;-

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Inna allatheena kafaroo yunfiqoona amwalahum liyasuddoo AAan sabeeli Allahi fasayunfiqoonaha thumma takoonu AAalayhim hasratan thumma yughlaboona waallatheena kafaroo ila jahannama yuhsharoona

37.arabic: لِيَمِيزَ ٱللَّهُ ٱلْخَبِيثَ مِنَ ٱلطَّيِّبِ وَيَجْعَلَ ٱلْخَبِيثَ بَعْضَهُۥ عَلَىٰ بَعْضٍ فَيَرْكُمَهُۥ جَمِيعًا فَيَجْعَلَهُۥ فِى جَهَنَّمَ ۚ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ هُمُ ٱلْخَـٰسِرُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: That God may separate the bad from the good, and link the wicked together and cast them into Hell. These are verily the people who will lose.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: In order that Allah may separate the filthy from the pure, and placing the filthy atop one another, make a heap and throw them into hell; it is they who are the losers.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: that God may distinguish the corrupt from the good, and place the corrupt one upon another, and so heap them up all together, and put them in Gehenna; those are the losers.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: In order that Allah may distinguish the vile from the good; and the vile He shall place one upon anot her, and shall pile it all together, and shall place it into Hell. Those! it is they who are the losers.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: In order that Allah may distinguish the wicked (disbelievers, polytheists and doers of evil deeds) from the good (believers of Islamic Monotheism and doers of righteous deeds), and put the wicked (disbelievers, polytheists and doers of evil deeds) one on another, heap them together and cast them into Hell. Those! it is they who are the losers.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: That God may distinguish the bad from the good, and heap the bad on top of one another, and pile them together, and throw them in Hell. These are the losers.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: so that Allah may separate the bad from the good, and join together all those who are bad into a pile one upon another, and cast them into Hell. They, it is they who are the losers.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: In order that Allah may distinguish the wicked from the good, and put the wicked one over another, heap them together and cast them into Hell. Those! it is they who are the losers.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: That Allah may separate the wicked from the good, The wicked will He place piece upon piece, and heap them all together, and consign them unto hell. Such verily are the losers.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: so that Allah may separate the bad ones from the good, and place the bad on one another, and pile them up together, and cast them into hell. It is they who are the losers.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: in order that Allah will separate the wicked from the good. He will heap the wicked one upon another, and then heap them together and cast them in Gehenna those shall be the losers.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: [This is] so that Allah may distinguish the wicked from the good and place the wicked some of them upon others and heap them all together and put them into Hell. It is those who are the losers.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: God will separate the wicked from the pure and will pile the wicked ones on top of one anothcr to be cast into hell. They are indeed lost.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: That Allah might separate the impure from the good, and put the impure, some of it upon the other, and pile it up together, then cast it into hell; these it is that are the losers.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: So that God may separate the bad from the good, He will heap the wicked one upon another and then cast them into Hell. These will surely be the losers.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: In order that Allah may separate the impure from the pure, put the impure, one on another, heap them together, and cast them into Hell. They will be the ones to have lost.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Liyameeza Allahu alkhabeetha mina alttayyibi wayajAAala alkhabeetha baAAdahu AAala baAAdin fayarkumahu jameeAAan fayajAAalahu fee jahannama olaika humu alkhasiroona

38.arabic: قُل لِّلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوٓا۟ إِن يَنتَهُوا۟ يُغْفَرْ لَهُم مَّا قَدْ سَلَفَ وَإِن يَعُودُوا۟ فَقَدْ مَضَتْ سُنَّتُ ٱلْأَوَّلِينَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: You tell the unbelievers in case they desist whatever has happened will be forgiven them. If they persist, they should remember the fate of those who have gone before them.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Say to the disbelievers that if they desist, what has passed will be forgiven to them; and if they do the same, the tradition of former people has already passed.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Say to the unbelievers, if they give over He will forgive them what is past; but if they return, the wont of the ancients is already gone!

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Say thou unto those who have disbelieved: if they desist, that which is past shall be forgiven them, and if they return, then already gone forth is the dispensation of the ancients.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Say to those who have disbelieved, if they cease (from disbelief) their past will be forgiven. But if they return (thereto), then the examples of those (punished) before them have already preceded (as a warning).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Say to those who disbelieve: if they desist, their past will be forgiven. But if they persist—the practice of the ancients has passed away.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: [O Prophet!] Tell the unbelievers that if they desist from evil, their past shall be forgiven and if they revert to their past ways, then it is well known what happened with the people of the past.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Say to those who have disbelieved, if they cease, their past will be forgiven. But if they return (thereto), then the examples of those (punished) before them have already preceded (as a warning).

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Tell those who disbelieve that if they cease (from persecution of believers) that which is past will be forgiven them; but if they return (thereto) then the example of the men of old hath already gone (before them, for a warning).

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Say to the faithless, if they cease [their belligerence against the Muslims], what is already past shall be forgiven them. But if they resume [their hostilities], then the precedent of the predecessors has already come to pass.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Tell the unbelievers that if they abandon their ways He will forgive them what is past; but, if they return, that was indeed the way of their forefathers who have passed away.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Say to those who have disbelieved [that] if they cease, what has previously occurred will be forgiven for them. But if they return [to hostility] - then the precedent of the former [rebellious] peoples has already taken place.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Muhammad, tell the unbelievers, if you give up your evil behavior, God will forgive whatever you have done in the past. But if you transgras again, your fate will be the same as that of those (unbelievers) who lived before you."

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Say to those who disbelieve, if they desist, that which is past shall be forgiven to them; and if they return, then what happened to the ancients has already passed.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Tell those who are bent on denying the truth that if they desist, their past shall be forgiven, but if they persist in sin, they have an example in the fate of those who went before.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Say to the Unbelievers, if (now) they desist (from Unbelief), their past would be forgiven them; but if they persist, the punishment of those before them is already (a matter of warning for them).

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Qul lillatheena kafaroo in yantahoo yughfar lahum ma qad salafa wain yaAAoodoo faqad madat sunnatu alawwaleena

39.arabic: وَقَـٰتِلُوهُمْ حَتَّىٰ لَا تَكُونَ فِتْنَةٌ وَيَكُونَ ٱلدِّينُ كُلُّهُۥ لِلَّهِ ۚ فَإِنِ ٱنتَهَوْا۟ فَإِنَّ ٱللَّهَ بِمَا يَعْمَلُونَ بَصِيرٌ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: So, fight them till all opposition ends, and obedience is wholly God's. If they desist then verily God sees all they do.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And fight them until no mischief remains and the entire religion is only for Allah; then if they desist, Allah sees all what they do.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Fight them, till there is no persecution and the religion is God's entirely; then if they give over, surely God sees the things they do;

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And fight against them until there be no temptations and their obedience be wholly unto Allah. So if they desist, thens verily Allah is the Beholder of that which they do.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And fight them until there is no more Fitnah (disbelief and polytheism: i.e. worshipping others besides Allah) and the religion (worship) will all be for Allah Alone [in the whole of the world]. But if they cease (worshipping others besides Allah), then certainly, Allah is All-Seer of what they do.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Fight them until there is no more persecution, and religion becomes exclusively for God. But if they desist—God is Seeing of what they do.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: And fight against them until the mischief ends and the way prescribed by Allah - the whole of it -prevail Then, if they give up mischief, surely Allah sees what they do.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And fight them until there is no more Fitnah, and the religion (worship) will all be for Allah alone. But if they cease, then certainly, Allah is All-Seer of what they do.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And fight them until persecution is no more, and religion is all for Allah. But if they cease, then lo! Allah is Seer of what they do.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Fight them until persecution is no more, and religion becomes exclusively for Allah. So if they desist, Allah indeed watches what they do.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Fight them until persecution is no more and the Religion of Allah reigns supreme. If they desist, Allah sees the things they do;

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And fight them until there is no fitnah and [until] the religion, all of it, is for Allah. And if they cease - then indeed, Allah is Seeing of what they do.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Fight them so that idolatry will not exist any more and God's religion will stand supreme. If theygive up the idols), God will be Well Aware of what they do.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And fight with them until there is no more persecution and religion should be only for Allah; but if they desist, then surely Allah sees what they do.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Fight them until there is no more [religious] persecution, and religion belongs wholly to God: if they desist, then surely God is watchful of what they do,

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: And fight them on until there is no more tumult or oppression, and there prevail justice and faith in Allah altogether and everywhere; but if they cease, verily Allah doth see all that they do.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Waqatiloohum hatta la takoona fitnatun wayakoona alddeenu kulluhu lillahi faini intahaw fainna Allaha bima yaAAmaloona baseerun

40.arabic: وَإِن تَوَلَّوْا۟ فَٱعْلَمُوٓا۟ أَنَّ ٱللَّهَ مَوْلَىٰكُمْ ۚ نِعْمَ ٱلْمَوْلَىٰ وَنِعْمَ ٱلنَّصِيرُ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: But if they are obstinate, know that God is your helper and protector: How excellent a helper, and how excellent a protector is He!

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And if they turn away, then know that Allah is your Master; so what an Excellent Master and what an Excellent Supporter!

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: but if they turn away, know that God is your Protector -- an excellent Protector, an excellent Helper!

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And if they turn away, then know that Allah is your Patron: excellent Patron! and excellent helper!

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And if they turn away, then know that Allah is your Maula (Patron, Lord, Protector and Supporter, etc.), (what) an Excellent Maula, and (what) an Excellent Helper!

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And if they turn away, know that God is your Protector. The Best Protector, and the Best Supporter.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: But if they turn away, then know well that Allah is your Protector - an excellent Protector and an excellent Helper.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And if they turn away, then know that Allah is your protector -- (what) an excellent protector and (what) an excellent helper!

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And if they turn away, then know that Allah is your Befriender - a Transcendent Patron, a Transcendent Helper!

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: But if they turn away, then know that Allah is your master: an excellent master and an excellent helper!

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: but if they turn away, know that Allah is your guardian, (He is) the best Guardian and the best Giver of victory.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: But if they turn away - then know that Allah is your protector. Excellent is the protector, and Excellent is the helper.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: If the (unbelievers) turn away from the faith, God is you (believers) best Guardian and best Helper.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And if they turn back, then know that Allah is your Patron; most excellent is the Patron and most excellent the Helper.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: but if they turn away, know that God is your Protector; the Best of Protectors and the Best of Helpers!

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: If they refuse, be sure that Allah is your Protector - the best to protect and the best to help.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Wain tawallaw faiAAlamoo anna Allaha mawlakum niAAma almawla waniAAma alnnaseeru

41.arabic: وَٱعْلَمُوٓا۟ أَنَّمَا غَنِمْتُم مِّن شَىْءٍ فَأَنَّ لِلَّهِ خُمُسَهُۥ وَلِلرَّسُولِ وَلِذِى ٱلْقُرْبَىٰ وَٱلْيَتَـٰمَىٰ وَٱلْمَسَـٰكِينِ وَٱبْنِ ٱلسَّبِيلِ إِن كُنتُمْ ءَامَنتُم بِٱللَّهِ وَمَآ أَنزَلْنَا عَلَىٰ عَبْدِنَا يَوْمَ ٱلْفُرْقَانِ يَوْمَ ٱلْتَقَى ٱلْجَمْعَانِ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ قَدِيرٌ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Know that one-fifth of what you acquire as booty (of war) is for God and His Apostle, and for relatives and orphans, the poor and wayfarers, if you truly believe in God and what We revealed to Our votary on the day of victory over the infidels when the two armies clashed (at Badr). For God has the power to do any thing.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And know that whatever you take as war booty, a fifth of that belongs to Allah and His Noble Messenger, and to relatives, and orphans, and the needy, and the traveller – if you have accepted faith in Allah and what We sent down to Our bondman on the decisive day – the day when two armies had met; and Allah is Able to do all things.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Know that, whatever booty you take, the fifth of it is God's, and the Messenger's, and the near kinsman's, and the orphans', and for the needy, and the traveller, if you believe in God and that We sent down upon Our servant on the day of salvation,. the day the two hosts encountered; and God is powerful over everything;

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And know that whatsoever ye obtain of spoils then verily unto Allah belongeth a fifth thereof and unto the apostle and unto his kindreds and the orphans and the needy and the wayfarer if ye indeed have believed in Allah and that which We sent down on our bondmans on the day of distinction, the day whereon the two hosts met. And Allah is over everything Potent.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And know that whatever of war-booty that you may gain, verily one-fifth (1/5th) of it is assigned to Allah, and to the Messenger, and to the near relatives [of the Messenger (Muhammad SAW)], (and also) the orphans, Al-Masakin (the poor) and the wayfarer, if you have believed in Allah and in that which We sent down to Our slave (Muhammad SAW) on the Day of criterion (between right and wrong), the Day when the two forces met (the battle of Badr) - And Allah is Able to do all things.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And know that whatever spoils you gain, to God belongs its fifth, and to the Messenger, and the relatives, and the orphans, and the poor, and to the wayfarer, provided you believe in God and in what We revealed to Our servant on the Day of Distinction, the day when the two armies met. God is Capable of everything.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Know that one fifth of the spoils that you obtain belongs to Allah, to the Messenger, to the near of kin, to the orphans, and the needy, and the wayfarer. This you must observe if you truly believe In Allah and in what We sent down on Our servant on the day when the true was distinguished from the false, the day on which the two armies met in battle. Allah has power over all things.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And know that whatever of war booty that you may gain, verily, one-fifth of it is assigned to Allah, and to the Messenger, and to the near relatives (of the Messenger), the orphans, the poor, and the wayfarer, if you have believed in Allah and in that which We sent down to Our servant on the Day of Criterion, the Day when the two forces met; and Allah is able to do all things.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And know that whatever ye take as spoils of war, lo! a fifth thereof is for Allah, and for the messenger and for the kinsman (who hath need) and orphans and the needy and the wayfarer, if ye believe in Allah and that which We revealed unto Our slave on the Day of Discrimination, the day when the two armies met. And Allah is Able to do all things.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Know that whatever thing you may come by, a fifth of it is for Allah and the Apostle, for the relatives and the orphans, for the needy and the traveller, if you have faith in Allah and what We sent down to Our servant on the Day of Separation, the day when the two hosts met; and Allah has power over all things.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: And know that one fifth of whatever you take as spoils belong to Allah, the Messenger, kinsmen of the Messenger, the orphans, the needy, and the destitute traveler; if you believe in Allah and what We sent down to Our worshiper on the day of victory, the day when the two armies met. Allah has power over all things.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And know that anything you obtain of war booty - then indeed, for Allah is one fifth of it and for the Messenger and for [his] near relatives and the orphans, the needy, and the [stranded] traveler, if you have believed in Allah and in that which We sent down to Our Servant on the day of criterion - the day when the two armies met. And Allah, over all things, is competent.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Know that whatever property you may gain, one fifth belongs to God, the Messenger, the kindred, orphans, the needy and those who need money while on a journey. (This is the law) if you believe in God and what We revealed to Our Servant on the Day of Distinction (Badr) when the armies confronted each other. God has power over all things.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And know that whatever thing you gain, a fifth of it is for Allah and for the Apostle and for the near of kin and the orphans and the needy and the wayfarer, if you believe in Allah and in that which We revealed to Our servant, on the day of distinction, the day on which the two parties met; and Allah has power over all things.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Know that one-fifth of your battle gains belongs to God and the Messenger, to his close relatives and orphans, to the needy and travellers, if you believe in God and the revelation We sent down to Our servant on the Decisive Day, the day when the two forces met. God has power over all things.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: And know that out of all the booty that ye may acquire (in war), a fifth share is assigned to Allah,- and to the Messenger, and to near relatives, orphans, the needy, and the wayfarer,- if ye do believe in Allah and in the revelation We sent down to Our servant on the Day of Testing,- the Day of the meeting of the two forces. For Allah hath power over all things.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: WaiAAlamoo annama ghanimtum min shayin faanna lillahi khumusahu walilrrasooli walithee alqurba waalyatama waalmasakeeni waibni alssabeeli in kuntum amantum biAllahi wama anzalna AAala AAabdina yawma alfurqani yawma iltaqa aljamAAani waAllahu AAala kulli shayin qadeerun

42.arabic: إِذْ أَنتُم بِٱلْعُدْوَةِ ٱلدُّنْيَا وَهُم بِٱلْعُدْوَةِ ٱلْقُصْوَىٰ وَٱلرَّكْبُ أَسْفَلَ مِنكُمْ ۚ وَلَوْ تَوَاعَدتُّمْ لَٱخْتَلَفْتُمْ فِى ٱلْمِيعَـٰدِ ۙ وَلَـٰكِن لِّيَقْضِىَ ٱللَّهُ أَمْرًا كَانَ مَفْعُولًا لِّيَهْلِكَ مَنْ هَلَكَ عَنۢ بَيِّنَةٍ وَيَحْيَىٰ مَنْ حَىَّ عَنۢ بَيِّنَةٍ ۗ وَإِنَّ ٱللَّهَ لَسَمِيعٌ عَلِيمٌ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: (That day) when you were at one end of the valley, (the unbelievers) at the other, and the caravan below you (on the lowland by the coast), you would surely have declined to fight if (the Makkans) had offered you battle. (But the battle did take place) that God may end the matter which had been accomplished, so that he who had to die may perish after a clear demonstration, and he who had to live may survive in the light of positive proof, for God hears all and knows everything.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: When you were on the near bank and the disbelievers on the far bank, and the caravan below you; and had you made an agreement between one another, you would have failed to reach on the appointed time – but this is in order that Allah may complete a thing that must be done – that he who dies may die by a clear proof and he who lives may live by a clear proof; and indeed Allah is surely, All Hearing, All Knowing.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: when you were on the nearer bank, and they were on the farther bank, and the cavalcade was below you; and had you made tryst together, you would have surely failed the tryst; but that God might determine a matter that was done, that whosoever perished might perish by a clear sign, and by a clear sign he might live who lived; and surely God is All-hearing, All-knowing.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And recall what time ye were on the hither side and they were on the yonder side and the caravan below you. And if ye had mutually appointed ye would surely have failed the appointment. But the action wao brought about in order that Allah may decree an affair already enacted, so that he who was to perish, should perish after an evidence and he who was to remain alive may remain alive after an evidence. And verily Allah is Hearing, Knowing.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
