translations.text: (And remember) when you (the Muslim army) were on the near side of the valley, and they on the farther side, and the caravan on the ground lower than you. Even if you had made a mutual appointment to meet, you would certainly have failed in the appointment, but (you met) that Allah might accomplish a matter already ordained (in His Knowledge); so that those who were to be destroyed (for their rejecting the Faith) might be destroyed after a clear evidence, and those who were to live (i.e. believers) might live after a clear evidence. And surely, Allah is All-Hearer, All-Knower.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Recall when you were on the nearer bank, and they were on the further bank, and the caravan was below you. Had you planned for this meeting, you would have disagreed on the timing, but God was to carry out a predetermined matter, so that those who perish would perish by clear evidence, and those who survive would survive by clear evidence. God is Hearing and Knowing.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: And recall when you were encamped at the nearer end of the valley (of Badr) and they were at the farther end and the caravan below you (along the seaside). Had you made a mutual appointment to meet in encounter, you would have declined. But encounter was brought about so that Allah might accomplish what He had decreed, and that he who was to perish should perish through a clear proof, and who was to survive might survive through a clear proof. Surely Allah is All-Hearing, All-Knowing.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: (And remember) when you (the Muslim army) were on the near side of the valley, and they on the farther side, and the caravan on the ground lower than you. Even if you had made a mutual appointment to meet, you would certainly have failed in the appointment, but (you met) that Allah might accomplish a matter already ordained (in His knowledge), so that those who were to be destroyed (for rejecting the faith) might be destroyed after a clear evidence, and those who were to live (believers) might live after a clear evidence. And surely, Allah is All-Hearer, All-Knower.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: When ye were on the near bank (of the valley) and they were on the yonder bank, and the caravan was below you (on the coast plain). And had ye trysted to meet one another ye surely would have failed to keep the tryst, but (it happened, as it did, without the forethought of either of you) that Allah might conclude a thing that must be done; that he who perished (on that day) might perish by a clear proof (of His Sovereignty) and he who survived might survive by a clear proof (of His Sovereignty). Lo! Allah in truth is Hearer, Knower.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: When you were on the nearer side, and they on the farther side, while the caravan was below you, and had you agreed together on an encounter, you would have certainly failed to keep the tryst, but in order that Allah may carry through a matter that was bound to be fulfilled, so that he who perishes might perish by a manifest proof, and he who lives may live on by a manifest proof, and Allah is indeed all-hearing, all-knowing.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: And when you were encamped on the nearside and they (the unbelievers) on the farther side, with the caravan below, had you made an appointment with them, you would have surely failed to keep it; but Allah sought to accomplish what He had ordained, so that by clear evidence, he who was destined to perish might perish, and he who was destined to live might live. Surely, Allah is Hearing, Knowing.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: [Remember] when you were on the near side of the valley, and they were on the farther side, and the caravan was lower [in position] than you. If you had made an appointment [to meet], you would have missed the appointment. But [it was] so that Allah might accomplish a matter already destined - that those who perished [through disbelief] would perish upon evidence and those who lived [in faith] would live upon evidence; and indeed, Allah is Hearing and Knowing.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Recall when your army was positioned at the less defensible brink of the valley, (the pagans') army had the more defensible higher side of the valley and the caravan was led (out of your reach) below. This situation did not take place according to your previous plans, otherwise, everything would have been different. (It was God's plan) to place you in a vulnerable position, exposed to the enemy and it was His plan to lead the caravan out of your reach) so that His decree that you would be granted a victory by a miracle would become a doubtless fact and so that those who were to be destroyed would face destruction with a clear knowledge of the Truth and those who were to survive would also survive with a clear knowledge of the Truth. God is All-hearing and All-knowing.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: When you were on the nearer side (of the valley) and they were on the farthest side, while the caravan was in a lower place than you; and if you had mutually made an appointment, you would certainly have broken away from the appointment, but-- in order that Allah might bring about a matter which was to be done, that he who would perish might perish by clear proof, and he who would live might live by clear proof; and most surely Allah is Hearing, Knowing;

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: You were on the nearer side of the valley, and they were on the farther side, and the caravan was below you. Had you wished to set a time by mutual agreement, you would certainly have disagreed on the timing. However, the encounter took place, so that God might settle a matter which had already been ordained, so that he who was to perish might perish after clear evidence of the truth, and he who was to live might live in clear evidence of the truth. Surely, God is all hearing and all-knowing.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Remember ye were on the hither side of the valley, and they on the farther side, and the caravan on lower ground than ye. Even if ye had made a mutual appointment to meet, ye would certainly have failed in the appointment: But (thus ye met), that Allah might accomplish a matter already enacted; that those who died might die after a clear Sign (had been given), and those who lived might live after a Clear Sign (had been given). And verily Allah is He Who heareth and knoweth (all things).

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Ith antum bialAAudwati alddunya wahum bialAAudwati alquswa waalrrakbu asfala minkum walaw tawaAAadtum laikhtalaftum fee almeeAAadi walakin liyaqdiya Allahu amran kana mafAAoolan liyahlika man halaka AAan bayyinatin wayahya man hayya AAan bayyinatin wainna Allaha lasameeAAun AAaleemun

43.arabic: إِذْ يُرِيكَهُمُ ٱللَّهُ فِى مَنَامِكَ قَلِيلًا ۖ وَلَوْ أَرَىٰكَهُمْ كَثِيرًا لَّفَشِلْتُمْ وَلَتَنَـٰزَعْتُمْ فِى ٱلْأَمْرِ وَلَـٰكِنَّ ٱللَّهَ سَلَّمَ ۗ إِنَّهُۥ عَلِيمٌۢ بِذَاتِ ٱلصُّدُورِ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: God showed (the Makkans) to be few in your dream, for if He had shown them to be many you would surely have lost courage and disagreed about the (wisdom) of the battle. But God spared you this, for He surely knows what is in the hearts of men.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: When O dear Prophet, Allah used to show the disbelievers in your dream as only a few*; and O Muslims, had He shown them to you as many, you would have certainly lost courage and disputed over the affair, but Allah rescued (you); indeed He knows what lies within the hearts. (* They numbered more but their actual strength was equal to only a few.)

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: When God showed thee them in thy dream as few; and had He shown them as many you would have lost heart, and quarrelled about the matter; but God saved; He knows the thoughts in the breasts.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And recall what time Allah shewed them few unto thee in thy dream. And had He shewn them numerous unto thee, surely ye would have flagged and surely ye would have disputed over the affair but Allah saved you. Verily He is the Knower of that which is in the breasts.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: (And remember) when Allah showed them to you as few in your (i.e. Muhammad's SAW) dream, if He had shown them to you as many, you would surely have been discouraged, and you would surely have disputed in making a decision. But Allah saved (you). Certainly, He is the All-Knower of what is in the breasts.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: God made them appear in your dream as few. Had He made them appear as many, you would have lost heart, and disputed in the matter. But God saved the situation. He knows what the hearts contain.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: And recall when Allah showed them to you in your dream to be few in number. And had He showed them to you to be numerous, you would have flagged and disagreed with one another about fighting them. But Allah saved you. Surely Allah knows what is hidden in the breasts.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: (And remember) when Allah showed them to you as few in your dream; if He had shown them to you as many, you would surely have been discouraged, and you would surely have disputed in making a decision. But Allah saved (you). Certainly, He is the All-Knower of that is in the breasts.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: When Allah showed them unto thee (O Muhammad) in thy dream as few in number, and if He had shown them to thee as many, ye (Muslims) would have faltered and would have quarrelled over the affair. But Allah saved (you). Lo! He knoweth what is in the breasts (of men).

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: When Allah showed them to you as few in your dream, and had He shown them as many, you would have lost heart, and disputed about the matter. But Allah spared you. Indeed He knows well what is in the breasts.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: And when Allah made them appear to you in a vision as a small band, had He showed them to you as many, your courage would have failed you and you would have quarreled over the affair. But Allah saved; He knows the inner most thoughts in the chests.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: [Remember, O Muhammad], when Allah showed them to you in your dream as few; and if He had shown them to you as many, you [believers] would have lost courage and would have disputed in the matter [of whether to fight], but Allah saved [you from that]. Indeed, He is Knowing of that within the breasts.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: In your dream, God showed (the pagans' army) as being only a few in number, for if He had showed them as a great number, you would have lost courage and would have started to quarrel among yourselves concerning this matter. But God saved you from that condition; He knows what is in your hearts.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: When Allah showed them to you in your dream as few; and if He had shown them to you as many you would certainly have become weak-hearted and you would have disputed about the matter, but Allah saved (you); surely He is the Knower of what is in the breasts.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: God showed them to you in your dream as small in number. If He had shown them to you as many, you would have lost heart and disputed about the matter; but God saved you. He has full knowledge of what is in the human heart.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Remember in thy dream Allah showed them to thee as few: if He had shown them to thee as many, ye would surely have been discouraged, and ye would surely have disputed in (your) decision; but Allah saved (you): for He knoweth well the (secrets) of (all) hearts.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Ith yureekahumu Allahu fee manamika qaleelan walaw arakahum katheeran lafashiltum walatanazaAAtum fee alamri walakinna Allaha sallama innahu AAaleemun bithati alssudoori

44.arabic: وَإِذْ يُرِيكُمُوهُمْ إِذِ ٱلْتَقَيْتُمْ فِىٓ أَعْيُنِكُمْ قَلِيلًا وَيُقَلِّلُكُمْ فِىٓ أَعْيُنِهِمْ لِيَقْضِىَ ٱللَّهُ أَمْرًا كَانَ مَفْعُولًا ۗ وَإِلَى ٱللَّهِ تُرْجَعُ ٱلْأُمُورُ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: When you faced them He made (the enemy) seem few to you in numbers, and made you appear fewer in their eyes, (it was) so that God could accomplish what had been decreed; for all things rest with God.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And when at the time of fighting He made the disbelievers seem few to you, and you as few in their sight, in order for Allah to conclude the matter that must be done; and towards Allah is the return of all matters.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: When God showed you them in your eyes as few, when you encountered, and made you few in their eyes, that God might determine a matter that was done; and unto God all matters are returned.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And recall what time He shewed them few in your eyes when ye met and lessened you in their eyes in order that Allah might decree an affair already enacted; and unto Allah are all affairs returned.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: appear And (remember) when you met (the army of the disbelievers on the Day of the battle of Badr), He showed them to you as few in your eyes and He made you as few in their eyes, so that Allah might accomplish a matter already ordained (in His Knowledge), and to Allah return all matters (for decision).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: When you met, He made them appear as few in your eyes, and made you appear fewer in their eyes, so that God may conclude a predetermined matter. To God all matters revert.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: And recall when He made them appear as few in your eyes when you met them in the battle just as He lessened you in their eyes so that Allah might accomplish what had been decreed. To Allah are all matters referred for decision.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And (remember) when you met, He showed them to you as few in your eyes and He made you appear as few in their eyes, so that Allah might accomplish a matter already ordained, and to Allah return all matters (for decision).

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And when He made you (Muslims), when ye met (them), see them with your eyes as few, and lessened you in their eyes, (it was) that Allah might conclude a thing that must be done. Unto Allah all things are brought back.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: And when He showed them to you as few in your eyes, when you met them [on the battlefield], and He made you [appear] few in their eyes, [it was] in order that Allah may carry through a matter that was bound to be fulfilled, and to Allah all matters are returned.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: And when you met them, He showed them in your eyes as being few, and decreased (your number) in their eyes so that Allah might determine what was ordained. To Allah all matters return.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And [remember] when He showed them to you, when you met, as few in your eyes, and He made you [appear] as few in their eyes so that Allah might accomplish a matter already destined. And to Allah are [all] matters returned.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: When you met the pagans' army, God made them appear fewer in your eyes and you appear fewer in their eyes so that His miracle of granting you (an incredible) victory could easily be fulfilled. To God do all things return.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And when He showed them to you, when you met, as few in your eyes and He made you to appear little in their eyes, in order that Allah might bring about a matter which was to be done, and to Allah are all affairs returned.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: When at the time of your encounter He made them appear few in your eyes, and made you appear few in their eyes, it was so that God might bring about that which had been decreed. Everything returns to God.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: And remember when ye met, He showed them to you as few in your eyes, and He made you appear as contemptible in their eyes: that Allah might accomplish a matter already enacted. For to Allah do all questions go back (for decision).

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Waith yureekumoohum ithi iltaqaytum fee aAAyunikum qaleelan wayuqallilukum fee aAAyunihim liyaqdiya Allahu amran kana mafAAoolan waila Allahi turjaAAu alomooru

45.arabic: يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوٓا۟ إِذَا لَقِيتُمْ فِئَةً فَٱثْبُتُوا۟ وَٱذْكُرُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ كَثِيرًا لَّعَلَّكُمْ تُفْلِحُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: O believers, when you meet an army, stand firm, and think of God a great deal that you may be blessed with success.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: O People who Believe! When you meet an army, hold firm and remember Allah profusely, in order that you succeed.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: O believers, whensoever you encounter a host, then stand firm, and remember God frequently; haply so you will prosper.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: O Ye who believe! when ye meet a party, stand firm and remember Allah much, that haply ye may fare well.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: O you who believe! When you meet (an enemy) force, take a firm stand against them and remember the Name of Allah much (both with tongue and mind), so that you may be successful.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: O you who believe! When you meet a force, stand firm, and remember God much, so that you may prevail.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Believers! When you encounter a host in battle, stand firm and remember Allah much that you may triumph.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: O you who believe! When you meet (an enemy) force, take a firm stand against them and remember Allah much, so that you may be successful.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: O ye who believe! When ye meet an army, hold firm and think of Allah much, that ye may be successful.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: O you who have faith! When you meet a host [in battle], then stand firm, and remember Allah greatly so that you may be felicitous.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Believers, when you meet an army stand firm and remember Allah abundantly, in order that you are prosperous.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: O you who have believed, when you encounter a company [from the enemy forces], stand firm and remember Allah much that you may be successful.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Believers, stand firm when you meet a band of your enemy and remember God often so that you may have everlasting happiness.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: O you who believe! when you meet a party, then be firm, and remember Allah much, that you may be successful.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Believers! When you encounter a party, remain firm and remember God much, so that you may succeed.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: O ye who believe! When ye meet a force, be firm, and call Allah in remembrance much (and often); that ye may prosper:

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Ya ayyuha allatheena amanoo itha laqeetum fiatan faothbutoo waothkuroo Allaha katheeran laAAallakum tuflihoona

46.arabic: وَأَطِيعُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ وَرَسُولَهُۥ وَلَا تَنَـٰزَعُوا۟ فَتَفْشَلُوا۟ وَتَذْهَبَ رِيحُكُمْ ۖ وَٱصْبِرُوٓا۟ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ مَعَ ٱلصَّـٰبِرِينَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Obey God and His Apostle, and do not disagree among yourselves or you will be unmanned and lose courage. Persevere, for God is with those who endure.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And obey Allah and His Noble Messenger, and do not dispute with one another for you will lose courage again and your strength will be lost, and patiently endure; indeed Allah is with those who patiently endure.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: And obey God, and His Messenger, and do not quarrel together, and so lose heart, and your power depart; and be patient; surely God is with the patient.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And obey Allah and His apostle, and dispute not, lest ye flag and your predominance depart, and be patient. Verily Allah is with the patient

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And obey Allah and His Messenger, and do not dispute (with one another) lest you lose courage and your strength depart, and be patient. Surely, Allah is with those who are As-Sabirin (the patient ones, etc.).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And obey God and His Messenger, and do not dispute, lest you falter and lose your courage. And be steadfast. God is with the steadfast.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: And obey Allah and His Messenger, and do not quarrel with one another lest you should lose courage and your power depart. Be steadfast, surely Allah is with those who remain steadfast.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And obey Allah and His Messenger, and do not dispute (with one another) lest you lose courage and your strength departs, and be patient. Surely, Allah is with the patients.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And obey Allah and His messenger, and dispute not one with another lest ye falter and your strength depart from you; but be steadfast! Lo! Allah is with the steadfast.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: And obey Allah and His Apostle, and do not dispute, or you will lose heart and your power will be gone. And be patient; indeed Allah is with the patient.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Obey Allah and His Messenger and do not dispute with one another lest you should lose courage and your resolve weaken. Have patience Allah is with those who are patient.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And obey Allah and His Messenger, and do not dispute and [thus] lose courage and [then] your strength would depart; and be patient. Indeed, Allah is with the patient.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Obey God and His Messenger. Do not quarrel with each other lest you fail or lose honor. Exercise patience; God is with those who have patience.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And obey Allah and His Apostle and do not quarrel for then you will be weak in hearts and your power will depart, and be patient; surely Allah is with the patient.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Obey God and His Messenger, and avoid dissension, lest you falter and are no longer held in awe. Have patience: God is with those who are patient.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: And obey Allah and His Messenger; and fall into no disputes, lest ye lose heart and your power depart; and be patient and persevering: For Allah is with those who patiently persevere:

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: WaateeAAoo Allaha warasoolahu wala tanazaAAoo fatafshaloo watathhaba reehukum waisbiroo inna Allaha maAAa alssabireena

47.arabic: وَلَا تَكُونُوا۟ كَٱلَّذِينَ خَرَجُوا۟ مِن دِيَـٰرِهِم بَطَرًا وَرِئَآءَ ٱلنَّاسِ وَيَصُدُّونَ عَن سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ ۚ وَٱللَّهُ بِمَا يَعْمَلُونَ مُحِيطٌ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Do not be like those who went out of their homes full of their own importance, ostentatiously, trying to hinder others from the way of God. But God encompasses all they do.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And do not be like those who came out from their houses proudly, and to be seen by men, and they prevent people from Allah’s way; and all their actions are within Allah’s control.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Be not as those who went forth from their habitations swaggering boastfully to show off to men, and barring from God's way; and God encompasses the things they do.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And be not like unto those who came forth from their homes vaunting and to be seen of men and hindering others from the way of Allah; and Allah is the Ecompasser of that which they work.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And be not like those who come out of their homes boastfully and to be seen of men, and hinder (men) from the Path of Allah. and Allah is Muhitun (encircling and thoroughly comprehending) all that they do.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And do not be like those who left their homes boastfully, showing off before the people, and barring others from the path of God. God comprehends what they do.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: And be not like those who came forth from their homes exulting, with a desire to be seen of men, and hindering others from the way of Allah. Allah encompasses all that they do.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And be not like those who come out of their homes boastfully and to be seen of men, and hinder (men) from the path of Allah; and Allah is Muhit (eneompassing and thoroughly comprehending) all that they do.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Be not as those who came forth from their dwellings boastfully and to be seen of men, and debar (men) from the way of Allah, while Allah is surrounding all they do.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Do not be like those who left their homes vainly and to show off to the people, and to bar [other people] from the way of Allah, and Allah comprehends what they do.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Do not be like those who left their homes elated with insolence and showing off to people, barring others from the Path of Allah but Allah encompasses what they do.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And do not be like those who came forth from their homes insolently and to be seen by people and avert [them] from the way of Allah. And Allah is encompassing of what they do.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Do not be like those who marched out boastfully to show off their strength to people and hinder people from the way of God. God encompasses everyone's activities.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And be not like those who came forth from their homes in great exultation and to be seen of men, and (who) turn away from the way of Allah, and Allah comprehends what they do.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Do not be like those who left their homes full of conceit and showing off to others. They debar others from the path of God: but God has knowledge of all their actions.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: And be not like those who started from their homes insolently and to be seen of men, and to hinder (men) from the path of Allah: For Allah compasseth round about all that they do.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Wala takoonoo kaallatheena kharajoo min diyarihim bataran wariaa alnnasi wayasuddoona AAan sabeeli Allahi waAllahu bima yaAAmaloona muheetun

48.arabic: وَإِذْ زَيَّنَ لَهُمُ ٱلشَّيْطَـٰنُ أَعْمَـٰلَهُمْ وَقَالَ لَا غَالِبَ لَكُمُ ٱلْيَوْمَ مِنَ ٱلنَّاسِ وَإِنِّى جَارٌ لَّكُمْ ۖ فَلَمَّا تَرَآءَتِ ٱلْفِئَتَانِ نَكَصَ عَلَىٰ عَقِبَيْهِ وَقَالَ إِنِّى بَرِىٓءٌ مِّنكُمْ إِنِّىٓ أَرَىٰ مَا لَا تَرَوْنَ إِنِّىٓ أَخَافُ ٱللَّهَ ۚ وَٱللَّهُ شَدِيدُ ٱلْعِقَابِ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Satan made their deeds look alluring to them, and said: "None will prevail over you this day, for I shall be near at hand." Yet when the two armies appeared face to face, he turned back and fled, saying: "I am not with you, for I can see what you cannot perceive. I fear God, for His punishment is severe."

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And when Satan made their deeds seem good in their sight and said, “This day no one can overpower you, and you are under my protection”; so when the two armies came face to face, he scrambled back and said, “I am unconcerned with you – I can see what is not visible to you – I fear Allah”; and Allah’s punishment is severe.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: And when Satan decked out their deeds fair to them, and said, 'Today no man shall overcome you, for I shall be your neighbour.' But when the two hosts sighted each other, he withdrew upon his heels, saying, 'I am quit of you; for I see what you do not see. I fear God; and God is terrible in retribution.'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And recall what time the Satan made their works fair- seeming unto them and said: there is none of mankind to overcome you to- day, and verily I am your neighbour. Then when the two parties faced each other, he turned on his heels, and said: verily I am quit of you, verily I behold that which ye behold not; verily I fear Allah: and Allah severe in retribution.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And (remember) when Shaitan (Satan) made their (evil) deeds seem fair to them and said, "No one of mankind can overcome you this Day (of the battle of Badr) and verily, I am your neighbour (for each and every help)." But when the two forces came in sight of each other, he ran away and said "Verily, I have nothing to do with you. Verily! I see what you see not. Verily! I fear Allah for Allah is Severe in punishment."

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Satan made their deeds appear good to them, and said, “You cannot be defeated by any people today, and I am at your side.” But when the two armies came in sight of one another, he turned on his heels, and said, “I am innocent of you; I see what you do not see; I fear God; God is severe in punishment.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: And recall when Satan made their works seem fair to them and said: 'None shall overcome you today. and I am your supporter.' But when the two armies faced each other, he turned on his heels, and said: 'Surely I am quit of you for I behold that which you do not. Indeed I fear Allah, and Allah is stern in punishment.'

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And (remember) when Shaytan made their (evil) deeds seem fair to them and said, "No one of mankind can overcome you this day (of the battle of Badr) and verily, I am your neighbor (for each and every help)." But when the two forces came in sight of each other, he ran away and said "Verily, I have nothing to do with you. Verily, I see what you see not. Verily, I fear Allah for Allah is severe in punishment."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And when Satan made their deeds seem fair to them and said: No-one of mankind can conquer you this day, for I am your protector. But when the armies came in sight of one another, he took flight, saying: Lo! I am guiltless of you. Lo! I see that which ye see not. Lo! I fear Allah. And Allah is severe in punishment.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: When Satan made their deeds seem decorous to them, and said [to the faithless], ‘No one shall overcome you today from among all mankind, and I will stand by you.’ But when the two hosts sighted each other, he took to his heels, saying, ‘Indeed I am quit of you. I see what you do not see. Indeed I fear Allah, and Allah is severe in retribution.’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: And when satan made their foul deeds seem fair to them. He said: 'No one shall conquer you this day. I shall be your savior' But when the two armies came within sight of each other, he took to his heels, saying: 'I reject you, for I can see what you cannot. I fear Allah, Allah is Stern in retribution'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And [remember] when Satan made their deeds pleasing to them and said, "No one can overcome you today from among the people, and indeed, I am your protector." But when the two armies sighted each other, he turned on his heels and said, "Indeed, I am disassociated from you. Indeed, I see what you do not see; indeed I fear Allah. And Allah is severe in penalty."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Satan made their (pagans') deeds seem attractive to them and said to them, "No one today is more powerful than you and I am your supporter." But when the two armies confronted one another and the pagans were defeated, then satan betrayed his friends saying, "I am not with you any more; I see what you do not see and I am afraid of God." God is severe in His retribution.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And when the Shaitan made their works fair seeming to them, and said: No one can overcome you this day, and surely I am your protector: but when the two parties came in sight of each other he turned upon his heels, and said: Surely I am clear of you, surely I see what you do not see, surely I fear Allah; and Allah is severe in requiting (evil).

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Satan made their deeds seem fair to them and said, "None of the people shall prevail against you this day; I shall be your protector!" But when the two forces came in sight of each other, he turned on his heels, saying, "This is where I leave you: I see what you do not, and I fear God -- God is severe in His punishment."

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Remember Satan made their (sinful) acts seem alluring to them, and said: "No one among men can overcome you this day, while I am near to you": But when the two forces came in sight of each other, he turned on his heels, and said: "Lo! I am clear of you; lo! I see what ye see not; Lo! I fear Allah: for Allah is strict in punishment."

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Waith zayyana lahumu alshshaytanu aAAmalahum waqala la ghaliba lakumu alyawma mina alnnasi wainnee jarun lakum falamma taraati alfiatani nakasa AAala AAaqibayhi waqala innee bareeon minkum innee ara ma la tarawna innee akhafu Allaha waAllahu shadeedu alAAiqabi

49.arabic: إِذْ يَقُولُ ٱلْمُنَـٰفِقُونَ وَٱلَّذِينَ فِى قُلُوبِهِم مَّرَضٌ غَرَّ هَـٰٓؤُلَآءِ دِينُهُمْ ۗ وَمَن يَتَوَكَّلْ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ فَإِنَّ ٱللَّهَ عَزِيزٌ حَكِيمٌ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: The hypocrites, and those who had doubts in their hearts, said: "Their faith has misled them." But whoever places his trust in God will find God mighty and wise.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: When the hypocrites and those in whose hearts is a disease were saying, “These Muslims are proud of their religion”; and whoever trusts Allah, then indeed Allah is Almighty, Wise.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: When the hypocrites, and those in whose hearts was sickness, said, 'Their religion has deluded them'; but whosoever puts his trust in God, surely God is All-mighty, All-wise.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And recall what time the hypocrites and these in whose hearts was a disease said: their religion hath be gulfed those. And whosoever relieth on Allah then verily Allah is Mighty, Wise.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: When the hypocrites and those in whose hearts was a disease (of disbelief) said: "These people (Muslims) are deceived by their religion." But whoever puts his trust in Allah, then surely, Allah is All-Mighty, All-Wise.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: The hypocrites and those in whose hearts is sickness said, “Their religion has deluded these people.” But whoever puts his trust in God—God is Mighty and Wise.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: And recall when the hypocrites and those whose hearts were diseased said: 'Their faith has deluded these (believers). But he who puts his trust in Allah shall find Allah All-Mighty. All-Wise.'

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: When the hypocrites and those in whose hearts was a disease (of disbelief) said: "These people (Muslims) are deceived by their religion." But whoever puts his trust in Allah, then surely, Allah is All-Mighty, All-Wise.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: When the hypocrites and those in whose hearts is a disease said: Their religion hath deluded these. Whoso putteth his trust in Allah (will find that) lo! Allah is Mighty, Wise.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: When the hypocrites said, and [also] those in whose hearts is a sickness, ‘Their religion has deceived them.’ But whoever puts his trust in Allah, then Allah is indeed all-mighty, all-wise.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: And when the hypocrites and those in whose hearts was sickness said: 'Those, their religion has deceived them' But whosoever puts his trust in Allah, indeed Allah is Mighty, Wise.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: [Remember] when the hypocrites and those in whose hearts was disease said, "Their religion has deluded those [Muslims]." But whoever relies upon Allah - then indeed, Allah is Exalted in Might and Wise.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: The hypocrites and those whose hearts are sick, say, "The (believers') religion has deceived them." Those who trust in God will find Him Majestic and All-wise.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: When the hypocrites and those in whose hearts was disease said: Their religion has deceived them; and whoever trusts in Allah, then surely Allah is Mighty, Wise.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: The hypocrites and those whose hearts were perverted said, "These people [the believers] must be deluded by their religion." But he who places his trust in God [knows that], God is Almighty and Wise.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Lo! the hypocrites say, and those in whose hearts is a disease: "These people,- their religion has misled them." But if any trust in Allah, behold! Allah is Exalted in might, Wise.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Ith yaqoolu almunafiqoona waallatheena fee quloobihim maradun gharra haolai deenuhum waman yatawakkal AAala Allahi fainna Allaha AAazeezun hakeemun

50.arabic: وَلَوْ تَرَىٰٓ إِذْ يَتَوَفَّى ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ ۙ ٱلْمَلَـٰٓئِكَةُ يَضْرِبُونَ وُجُوهَهُمْ وَأَدْبَـٰرَهُمْ وَذُوقُوا۟ عَذَابَ ٱلْحَرِيقِ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: If you could only see the infidels as the angels draw away their souls and strike their faces and their backs, (saying): "Taste the torment of burning

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And if you see the angels when they are removing the souls of the disbelievers, hitting them on their faces and their backs; “Taste more of the punishment of the fire!” (Punishment in the grave is proven by this verse.)

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: If thou couldst only see when the angels take the unbelievers, beating their faces and their backs: 'Taste the chastisement of the burning --

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And couldst thou behold when the angels take away the life of those who disbelive striking their faces and their backs: taste the torment of burning.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And if you could see when the angels take away the souls of those who disbelieve (at death), they smite their faces and their backs, (saying): "Taste the punishment of the blazing Fire."

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: If only you could see, as the angels take away those who disbelieve, striking their faces and their backs: “Taste the agony of the Burning.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: And if you could only see when the angels took away the souls of the unbelievers, striking them on their faces and backs, saying: 'Taste the torment of burning.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And if you could see when the angels take away the souls of those who disbelieve (at death); they smite their faces and their backs, (saying): "Taste the punishment of the blazing Fire. "

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: If thou couldst see how the angels receive those who disbelieve, smiting faces and their backs and (saying): Taste the punishment of burning!

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Were you to see when the angels take away the faithless, striking their faces and their backs, [saying], ‘Taste the punishment of the burning.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: If you could see the angels when they carry off the souls of the unbelievers, beating their faces and their backs, saying: 'Taste the punishment of Burning!

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And if you could but see when the angels take the souls of those who disbelieved... They are striking their faces and their backs and [saying], "Taste the punishment of the Burning Fire.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Would that you could have seen the angels taking the souls of the unbelievers away from their bodies and smiting their faces and their backs saying, "Suffer the burning torment.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And had you seen when the angels will cause to die those who disbelieve, smiting their faces and their backs, and (saying): Taste the punishment of burning.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: If you could see, when the angels take the souls of those who are bent on denying the truth [at death], how they strike their faces and backs: saying "Taste the punishment of the Burning!

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: If thou couldst see, when the angels take the souls of the Unbelievers (at death), (How) they smite their faces and their backs, (saying): "Taste the penalty of the blazing Fire-

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Walaw tara ith yatawaffa allatheena kafaroo almalaikatu yadriboona wujoohahum waadbarahum wathooqoo AAathaba alhareeqi

51.arabic: ذَٰلِكَ بِمَا قَدَّمَتْ أَيْدِيكُمْ وَأَنَّ ٱللَّهَ لَيْسَ بِظَلَّـٰمٍ لِّلْعَبِيدِ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: For what you have brought upon yourselves." God is surely not unjust to His creatures, (they are unjust to themselves).

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: “This is the recompense of what your own hands have sent ahead, and Allah does not oppress His bondmen.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: that, for what your hands have forwarded, and for that God is never unjust unto His servants.'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: This, because of that which your hands have sent forth, and verily Allah is never unjust unto His creatures.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: "This is because of that which your hands had forwarded. And verily, Allah is not unjust to His slaves."

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: “That is because of what your hands have committed, and because God is not unjust to the servants.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: This is your punishment for what your hands wrought. Allah is not unjust in the least to His creatures.'

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: "This is because of that which your hands forwarded. And verily, Allah is not unjust to His servants."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: This is for that which your own hands have sent before (to the Judgment), and (know) that Allah is not a tyrant to His slaves.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: That is because of what your hands have sent ahead, and because Allah is not tyrannical to the servants.’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: That is for what your hands have forwarded, Allah is not unjust to His worshipers'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: That is for what your hands have put forth [of evil] and because Allah is not ever unjust to His servants."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: This is the result of their deeds. God is not unjust to His servants."

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: This is for what your own hands have sent on before, and because Allah is not in the least unjust to the servants;

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: This is the punishment for what your hands committed -- for, God never does the least wrong to His servants."

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: "Because of (the deeds) which your (own) hands sent forth; for Allah is never unjust to His servants:

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Thalika bima qaddamat aydeekum waanna Allaha laysa bithallamin lilAAabeedi

52.arabic: كَدَأْبِ ءَالِ فِرْعَوْنَ ۙ وَٱلَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِهِمْ ۚ كَفَرُوا۟ بِـَٔايَـٰتِ ٱللَّهِ فَأَخَذَهُمُ ٱللَّهُ بِذُنُوبِهِمْ ۗ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ قَوِىٌّ شَدِيدُ ٱلْعِقَابِ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Their case is like that of Pharaoh's people, and of those before them, who denied the revelations of God, and were punished for their sins by God, and God is all powerful and Severe His punishment.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Like the ways of Firaun’s people, and those before them; they disbelieved in the signs of Allah – therefore Allah seized them on account of their sins; indeed Allah is Most Powerful, Severe in Punishing.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Like Pharaoh's folk, and the people before him, who disbelieved in God's signs; God seized them because of their sins; God is strong, terrible in retribution.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Like are they unto the wont of the house of Fir'awn and those before them. They disbelieved in the signs of Allah, wherefore Allah laid hold of them for their sins. Verily Allah is strong, Severe in retribution.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Similar to the behaviour of the people of Fir'aun (Pharaoh), and of those before them; they rejected the Ayat (proofs, verses, etc.) of Allah, so Allah punished them for their sins. Verily, Allah is All-Strong, Severe in punishment.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Like the behavior of the people of Pharaoh, and those before them. They rejected the signs of God, so God seized them for their sins. God is Powerful, Severe in punishment.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Their case is like that of the people of Pharaoh and those before them. They denied the signs of Allah and so Allah seized them for their sins. Surely Allah is All-Powerful, Stern in retribution.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Similar to the behavior of the people of Fir`awn, and of those before them -- they rejected the Ayat of Allah, so Allah punished them for their sins. Verily, Allah is All-Strong, severe in punishment.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: (Their way is) as the way of Pharaoh's folk and those before them; they disbelieved the revelations of Allah, and Allah took them in their sins. Lo! Allah is Strong, severe in punishment.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Like the precedent of Pharaoh’s clan and those who were before them, who defied Allah’s signs, so Allah seized them for their sins. Indeed Allah is all-strong, severe in retribution.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Like Pharaoh's family and those who have gone before them, they disbelieved the signs of Allah. Therefore, Allah seized them in their sinfulness. Mighty is Allah and stern in retribution.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: [Theirs is] like the custom of the people of Pharaoh and of those before them. They disbelieved in the signs of Allah, so Allah seized them for their sins. Indeed, Allah is Powerful and severe in penalty.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: The same thing happened to the people of Pharaoh and those who lived before them. They rejected the revelations of God and, because of their sins, His retribution struck them. God is All-powerful and stern in His retribution.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: In the manner of the people of Firon and those before them; they disbelieved in Allah's communications, therefore Allah destroyed them on account of their faults; surely Allah is strong, severe in requiting (evil).

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Like Pharaoh's people and those that have gone before them, they rejected God's signs and God seized them for their sins. God is strong, and severe in punishment!

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: "(Deeds) after the manner of the people of Pharaoh and of those before them: They rejected the Signs of Allah, and Allah punished them for their crimes: for Allah is Strong, and Strict in punishment:

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Kadabi ali firAAawna waallatheena min qablihim kafaroo biayati Allahi faakhathahumu Allahu bithunoobihim inna Allaha qawiyyun shadeedu alAAiqabi

53.arabic: ذَٰلِكَ بِأَنَّ ٱللَّهَ لَمْ يَكُ مُغَيِّرًا نِّعْمَةً أَنْعَمَهَا عَلَىٰ قَوْمٍ حَتَّىٰ يُغَيِّرُوا۟ مَا بِأَنفُسِهِمْ ۙ وَأَنَّ ٱللَّهَ سَمِيعٌ عَلِيمٌ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: God does not withdraw a favour bestowed upon a people unless they change themselves, for God hears all and knows everything.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: This is because Allah does not change the favour He has bestowed upon any people until they first change themselves, and indeed Allah is All Hearing, All Knowing.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: That is because God would never change His favour that He conferred on a people until they changed what was within themselves; and that God is All-hearing, All-knowing.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: This, because Allah is not one to change His favour wherewith He hath favoured a people until they have changed that which is in themselves; and verily Allah is Hearing, Knowing.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: That is so because Allah will never change a grace which He has bestowed on a people until they change what is in their ownselves. And verily, Allah is All-Hearer, All-Knower.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: That is because God would never change a blessing He has bestowed on a people unless they change what is within themselves, and because God is Hearing and Knowing.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: This happened because Allah is not one to change the favour which He has bestowed upon a people until they have changed their attitude., Surely Allah is All-Hearing, All-Knowing.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: That is so because Allah will never change a grace which He has bestowed on a people until they change what is in themselves. And verily, Allah is All-Hearer, All-Knower.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: That is because Allah never changeth the grace He hath bestowed on any people until they first change that which is in their hearts, and (that is) because Allah is Hearer, Knower.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: That is because Allah never changes a blessing that He has bestowed on a people unless they change what is in their own souls, and Allah is all-hearing, all-knowing:

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: This is because Allah would never change His favor that He bestowed upon a nation until they change what is in their hearts. Allah is Hearing, Knowing.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: That is because Allah would not change a favor which He had bestowed upon a people until they change what is within themselves. And indeed, Allah is Hearing and Knowing.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: God does not change the favor that He has bestowed on a nation unless that nation changes what is in its soul. God is All-hearing and All-knowing.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: This is because Allah has never changed a favor which He has conferred upon a people until they change their own condition; and because Allah is Hearing, Knowing;

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: God would never withdraw a favour that He had conferred upon a people unless they change what is in their hearts. God is all hearing and all-knowing.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: "Because Allah will never change the grace which He hath bestowed on a people until they change what is in their (own) souls: and verily Allah is He Who heareth and knoweth (all things)."

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Thalika bianna Allaha lam yaku mughayyiran niAAmatan anAAamaha AAala qawmin hatta yughayyiroo ma bianfusihim waanna Allaha sameeAAun AAaleemun

54.arabic: كَدَأْبِ ءَالِ فِرْعَوْنَ ۙ وَٱلَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِهِمْ ۚ كَذَّبُوا۟ بِـَٔايَـٰتِ رَبِّهِمْ فَأَهْلَكْنَـٰهُم بِذُنُوبِهِمْ وَأَغْرَقْنَآ ءَالَ فِرْعَوْنَ ۚ وَكُلٌّ كَانُوا۟ ظَـٰلِمِينَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: This was the case with the people of Pharaoh and those before them, who rejected the signs of their Lord and were destroyed for their sins, and We drowned the people of Pharaoh as they were oppressors.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Like the ways of Firaun’s people and those before them; they denied the signs of Allah – We therefore destroyed them on account of their sins and We drowned the people of Firaun; and they all were unjust.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Like Pharaoh's folk, and the people before him, who cried lies to the signs of their Lord, so We destroyed them because of their sins, and We drowned the folk of Pharaoh; and all were evildoers.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Like are they Unto the wont of the house of Fir'awn and those before them. They belied the signs of their Lord; wherefore We destroyed them for their sins, and drowned the house of fir'awn; and all of them were wrong-doers.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Similar to the behaviour of the people of Fir'aun (Pharaoh), and those before them. They belied the Ayat (proofs, evidences, verses, lessons, signs, revelations, etc.), of their Lord, so We destroyed them for their sins, and We drowned the people of Fir'aun (Pharaoh) for they were all Zalimun (polytheists and wrong-doers, etc.).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Such was the case with the people of Pharaoh, and those before them. They denied the signs of their Lord, so We annihilated them for their wrongs, and We drowned the people of Pharaoh—they were all evildoers.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Their case is like that of the people of Pharaoh and those before them: they rejected the signs of their Lord as false and so We destroyed them for their sins, and caused the people of Pharaoh to drown. For they were wrong-doers all.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Similar to the behavior of the people of Fir`awn, and those before them. They belied the Ayat of their Lord, so We destroyed them for their sins, and We drowned the people of Fir`awn for they were all wrongdoers.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: (Their way is) as the way of Pharaoh's folk and those before them; they denied the revelations of their Lord, so We destroyed them in their sins. And We drowned the folk of Pharaoh. All were evil-doers.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Like the precedent of Pharaoh’s clan and those who were before them, who denied the signs of their Lord; so We destroyed them for their sins, and We drowned Pharaoh’s clan; and they were all wrongdoers.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Like Pharaoh's family and those who have gone before them, they belied the signs of their Lord, and so We destroyed them for their sins and drowned Pharaoh's family. They were all harmdoers.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: [Theirs is] like the custom of the people of Pharaoh and of those before them. They denied the signs of their Lord, so We destroyed them for their sins, and We drowned the people of Pharaoh. And all [of them] were wrongdoers.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Like the people of the Pharaoh and those who lived before them, (the unbelievers) rejected the revelations of God. We destroyed them for their sins and drowned the people of Pharaoh. They were all unjust.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: In the manner of the people of Firon and those before them; they rejected the communications of their Lord, therefore We destroyed them on account of their faults and We drowned Firon's people, and they were all unjust.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Like Pharaoh's people and those who went before them, they denied their Lord's signs: We destroyed them for their sins, and We drowned Pharaoh's people -- they were all evil-doers.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: (Deeds) after the manner of the people of Pharaoh and those before them": They treated as false the Signs of their Lord: so We destroyed them for their crimes, and We drowned the people of Pharaoh: for they were all oppressors and wrong-doers.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Kadabi ali firAAawna waallatheena min qablihim kaththaboo biayati rabbihim faahlaknahum bithunoobihim waaghraqna ala firAAawna wakullun kanoo thalimeena

55.arabic: إِنَّ شَرَّ ٱلدَّوَآبِّ عِندَ ٱللَّهِ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ فَهُمْ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Verily the worst of creatures in the sight of God are those who deny (the truth), and will not believe.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Indeed the worst beasts in the sight of Allah are the people who disbelieve and do not accept faith.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Surely the worst of beasts in God's sight are the unbelievers, who will not believe,

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Verily the vilest of moving creatures with Allah are those who disbelieve-wherefore they shall not believe-

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Verily, The worst of moving (living) creatures before Allah are those who disbelieve, - so they shall not believe.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: The worst of creatures in God's view are those who disbelieve. They have no faith.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Surely the worst moving creatures in the sight of Allah are those who definitively denied the truth and are therefore in no way prepared to accept it;

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Verily, the worst of living creatures before Allah are those who disbelieve, -- so they shall not believe.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Lo! the worst of beasts in Allah's sight are the ungrateful who will not believe.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Indeed the worst of beasts in Allah’s sight are those who are faithless; so they will not have faith.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Indeed, the basest creatures before Allah are the unbelievers for they will not believe;

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Indeed, the worst of living creatures in the sight of Allah are those who have disbelieved, and they will not [ever] believe -

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: The most wicked creatures in the sight of God are the unbelievers who never have faith,

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Surely the vilest of animals in Allah's sight are those who disbelieve, then they would not believe.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: The worst creatures in the sight of God are those who reject Him and will not believe;

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: For the worst of beasts in the sight of Allah are those who reject Him: They will not believe.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Inna sharra alddawabbi AAinda Allahi allatheena kafaroo fahum la yuminoona

56.arabic: ٱلَّذِينَ عَـٰهَدتَّ مِنْهُمْ ثُمَّ يَنقُضُونَ عَهْدَهُمْ فِى كُلِّ مَرَّةٍ وَهُمْ لَا يَتَّقُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: As for those with whom you have made a treaty and who abrogate it every time, and do not fear God,

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Those with whom you made a treaty, then they break their agreement each time and do not fear.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: those of them with whom thou hast made compact then they break their compact every time, not being godfearing.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: They with whom thou covenantedest, then they break their covenant every time, and they fear not.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: They are those with whom you made a covenant, but they break their covenant every time and they do not fear Allah.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Those of them with whom you made a treaty, but they violate their agreement every time. They are not righteous.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: (especially) those with whom you entered into a covenant and then they broke their covenant time after time, and who do not fear Allah.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: They are those with whom you made a covenant, but they break their covenant every time and they do not have Taqwa.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Those of them with whom thou madest a treaty, and then at every opportunity they break their treaty, and they keep not duty (to Allah).

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: —Those with whom you made a treaty, and who violated their treaty every time, and who are not Godwary.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: those with whom you have made a treaty, then break their treaty with you every time; having no fear (of Allah),

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: The ones with whom you made a treaty but then they break their pledge every time, and they do not fear Allah.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: who make promises but break them every time, and who have no piety.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Those with whom you make an agreement, then they break their agreement every time and they do not guard (against punishment).

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: those with whom you have made a covenant, and who break their covenant on every occasion and have no fear [of God].

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: They are those with whom thou didst make a covenant, but they break their covenant every time, and they have not the fear (of Allah).

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Allatheena AAahadta minhum thumma yanqudoona AAahdahum fee kulli marratin wahum la yattaqoona

57.arabic: فَإِمَّا تَثْقَفَنَّهُمْ فِى ٱلْحَرْبِ فَشَرِّدْ بِهِم مَّنْ خَلْفَهُمْ لَعَلَّهُمْ يَذَّكَّرُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: If you meet them in battle, inflict on them such a defeat as would be a lesson for those who come after them, and that they may be warned.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: So if you find them in battle, kill them in a manner which makes those behind them scamper back, in the hope that they may learn a lesson.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: So, if thou comest upon them anywhere in the war, deal with them in such wise as to scatter the ones behind them; haply they will remember.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Wherefore if thou over-takest them in war, disperse thou through them those behind them, that haply they may be admonished.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: So if you gain the mastery over them in war, punish them severely in order to disperse those who are behind them, so that they may learn a lesson.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: If you confront them in battle, make of them a fearsome example for those who follow them, that they may take heed.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: So if you meet them in war, make of them a fearsome example for those who follow them that they may he admonished.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: So if you gain the mastery over them in war, then disperse those who are behind them, so that they may learn a lesson.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: If thou comest on them in the war, deal with them so as to strike fear in those who are behind them, that haply they may remember.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: So if you confront them in battle, treat them [in such a wise] as to disperse those who are behind them, so that they may take admonition.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: if you meet them in battle, cause their example to scatter those behind them so that they remember.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: So if you, [O Muhammad], gain dominance over them in war, disperse by [means of] them those behind them that perhaps they will be reminded.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: When you capture the (unbelievers) during a fight, teach them a lesson so that they thereafter will always be aware of the threat of your power.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Therefore if you overtake them in fighting, then scatter by (making an example of) them those who are in their rear, that they may be mindful.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Should you encounter them in war, then deal with them in such a manner that those that follow them should abandon their designs and may take warning.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: If ye gain the mastery over them in war, disperse, with them, those who follow them, that they may remember.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Faimma tathqafannahum fee alharbi fasharrid bihim man khalfahum laAAallahum yaththakkaroona

58.arabic: وَإِمَّا تَخَافَنَّ مِن قَوْمٍ خِيَانَةً فَٱنۢبِذْ إِلَيْهِمْ عَلَىٰ سَوَآءٍ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ لَا يُحِبُّ ٱلْخَآئِنِينَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: If you apprehend treachery from a people (with whom you have a treaty), retaliate by breaking off (relations) with them, for God does not like those who are treacherous.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And if you apprehend treachery from a nation, then throw back their treaty towards them in reciprocity; indeed Allah does not like the treacherous.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: And if thou fearest treachery any way at the hands of a people, dissolve it with them equally; surely God loves not the treacherous.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And shouldst thou fear treachery from any people cast back then unto them their covenant to be equal. Verily Allah approveth not the treacherous.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: If you (O Muhammad SAW) fear treachery from any people throw back (their covenant) to them (so as to be) on equal terms (that there will be no more covenant between you and them). Certainly Allah likes not the treacherous.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: If you fear treachery on the part of a people, break off with them in a like manner. God does not like the treacherous.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: And if you fear treachery from any people (with whom you have a covenant) then publicly throw their covenant at them. Allah does not love the treacherous.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: If you fear treachery from any people, throw back (their covenant) to them (so as to be) on equal terms. Certainly Allah likes not the treacherous.) make a heading, Allah says to His Prophet,

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And if thou fearest treachery from any folk, then throw back to them (their treaty) fairly. Lo! Allah loveth not the treacherous.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: And if you fear treachery from a people, break off [the treaty] with them in a like manner. Indeed Allah does not like the treacherous.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: If you fear treachery from any of your allies, you can dissolve with them equally. Allah does not love the treacherous.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: If you [have reason to] fear from a people betrayal, throw [their treaty] back to them, [putting you] on equal terms. Indeed, Allah does not like traitors.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: If you are afraid of the treachery of some of your allies, you may disregard your treaty with them. God does not love the treacherous ones.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And if you fear treachery on the part of a people, then throw back to them on terms of equality; surely Allah does not love the treacherous.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: And if you learn of treachery on the part of any people, throw their treaty back at them, so as to be on equal terms, for God does not love the treacherous.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: If thou fearest treachery from any group, throw back (their covenant) to them, (so as to be) on equal terms: for Allah loveth not the treacherous.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Waimma takhafanna min qawmin khiyanatan fainbith ilayhim AAala sawain inna Allaha la yuhibbu alkhaineena

59.arabic: وَلَا يَحْسَبَنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ سَبَقُوٓا۟ ۚ إِنَّهُمْ لَا يُعْجِزُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: The infidels should not think that they can bypass (the law of God). Surely they cannot get away.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And never may the disbelievers pride that they have escaped; indeed they can never defeat.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: And thou art not to suppose that they who disbelieve have outstripped Me; they cannot frustrate My will.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Let not those who disbelieve deem that they have escaped, verily they cannot frustrate.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And let not those who disbelieve think that they can outstrip (escape from the punishment). Verily, they will never be able to save themselves (from Allah's Punishment).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Let not the disbelievers assume that they are ahead. They will not escape.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Let not the deniers of the truth be deluded that they will gain any advantage. Surely, they, can never overcome Us!

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And let not those who disbelieve think that they can outstrip (escape from the punishment). Verily, they will never be able to save themselves (from Allah's punishment).

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And let not those who disbelieve suppose that they can outstrip (Allah's Purpose). Lo! they cannot escape.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Let the faithless not suppose that they have outmanoeuvred [Allah]. Indeed, they cannot frustrate [His power].

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Do not suppose that the unbelievers have outstripped (Allah). They cannot frustrate Me.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And let not those who disbelieve think they will escape. Indeed, they will not cause failure [to Allah].

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: The unbelievers should not think that they can really escape Us or that they can never be defeated.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And let not those who disbelieve think that they shall come in first; surely they will not escape.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Let not the deniers think that they will ever get away. They cannot frustrate [God's purpose]. They have not the power to do so.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Let not the unbelievers think that they can get the better (of the godly): they will never frustrate (them).

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Wala yahsabanna allatheena kafaroo sabaqoo innahum la yuAAjizoona

60.arabic: وَأَعِدُّوا۟ لَهُم مَّا ٱسْتَطَعْتُم مِّن قُوَّةٍ وَمِن رِّبَاطِ ٱلْخَيْلِ تُرْهِبُونَ بِهِۦ عَدُوَّ ٱللَّهِ وَعَدُوَّكُمْ وَءَاخَرِينَ مِن دُونِهِمْ لَا تَعْلَمُونَهُمُ ٱللَّهُ يَعْلَمُهُمْ ۚ وَمَا تُنفِقُوا۟ مِن شَىْءٍ فِى سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ يُوَفَّ إِلَيْكُمْ وَأَنتُمْ لَا تُظْلَمُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Prepare against them whatever arms and cavalry you can muster, that you may strike terror in (the hearts of) the enemies of God and your own, and others besides them not known to you, but known to God. Whatever you spend in the way of God will be paid back to you in full, and no wrong will be done to you.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And keep ready for them the maximum of forces you can and the maximum number of horses you can keep tethered, in order to instil awe in the hearts of those who are the enemies of Allah and who are your enemies, and in the hearts of some others whom you do not know; Allah knows them; and whatever you spend in Allah's cause will be repaid to you in full and you will never be in a loss.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Make ready for them whatever force and strings of horses you can, to terrify thereby the enemy of God and your enemy, and others besides them that you know not; God knows them. And whatsoever you expend in the way of God shall be repaid you in full; you will not be wronged.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And get ready against them whatsoever ye can of force and wellfed horses whereby ye may overawe the enemy of Allah and your enemy and others besides them whom ye know not Allah knoweth them. And whatsoever ye expend in the way of Allah shall be repaid to you in full; and ye shall not be wronged.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And make ready against them all you can of power, including steeds of war (tanks, planes, missiles, artillery, etc.) to threaten the enemy of Allah and your enemy, and others besides whom, you may not know but whom Allah does know. And whatever you shall spend in the Cause of Allah shall be repaid unto you, and you shall not be treated unjustly.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And prepare against them all the power you can muster, and all the cavalry you can mobilize, to terrify thereby God’s enemies and your enemies, and others besides them whom you do not know, but God knows them. Whatever you spend in God’s way will be repaid to you in full, and you will not be wronged.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Make ready for an encounter against them all the forces and well-readied horses you can muster that you may overawe the enemies of Allah and your own enemies and others besides them of whom you are unaware but of whom Allah is aware. Whatever you may spend in the cause of Allah shall be fully repaid to you, and you shall not be wronged.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And make ready against them all you can of power, including steeds of war to threaten the enemy of Allah and your enemy, and others besides them, whom you may not know but whom Allah does know. And whatever you shall spend in the cause of Allah shall be repaid unto you, and you shall not be treated unjustly.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Make ready for them all thou canst of (armed) force and of horses tethered, that thereby ye may dismay the enemy of Allah and your enemy, and others beside them whom ye know not. Allah knoweth them. Whatsoever ye spend in the way of Allah it will be repaid to you in full, and ye will not be wronged.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Prepare against them whatever you can of [military] power and war-horses, awing thereby the enemy of Allah, and your enemy, and others besides them, whom you do not know, but Allah knows them. And whatever you spend in the way of Allah will be repaid to you in full, and you will not be wronged.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Muster against them whatever you are able of force and tethers (ropes) of horses, so that you strike terror into the enemies of Allah and your enemy, and others besides them whom you do not know but Allah does. All that you spend in the Way of Allah shall be repaid to you. You shall not be wronged.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And prepare against them whatever you are able of power and of steeds of war by which you may terrify the enemy of Allah and your enemy and others besides them whom you do not know [but] whom Allah knows. And whatever you spend in the cause of Allah will be fully repaid to you, and you will not be wronged.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Mobilize your (defensive) force as much as you can to frighten the enemies of God and your own enemies. This also will frighten those who are behind them whom you do not know but God knows well. Whatever you spend for the cause of God, He will give you sufficient recompense with due justice.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And prepare against them what force you can and horses tied at the frontier, to frighten thereby the enemy of Allah and your enemy and others besides them, whom you do not know (but) Allah knows them; and whatever thing you will spend in Allah's way, it will be paid back to you fully and you shall not be dealt with unjustly.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Prepare any strength you can muster against them, and any cavalry with which you can overawe God's enemy and your own enemy as well, and others besides them whom you do not know, but who are known to God. Anything you spend in the way of God will be repaid to you in full. You will not be wronged.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Against them make ready your strength to the utmost of your power, including steeds of war, to strike terror into (the hearts of) the enemies, of Allah and your enemies, and others besides, whom ye may not know, but whom Allah doth know. Whatever ye shall spend in the cause of Allah, shall be repaid unto you, and ye shall not be treated unjustly.
